---
title: "20260405_AIRE_Manuscript"
author: "AxakovaA"
date: '2026-04-26'
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r load_packages}
library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)
library(stringr)
library(dplyr)
library(mgsub)
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggVennDiagram)
library(forcats)
library(tibble)
library(ggvenn)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(zoo)
library(reshape2)
library(readr)
library(scales)
library(ggalluvial)
library(ggrepel)
```


```{r preliminary_data}
#this chunk generates Figure S2

#read data
df <- read.table("gate2mch.txt", header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
dfv1 <- df[df$vector == 1,]
dfv2 <- df[(df$vector == 2) & (df$day == 9),]
dfv2 <- dfv2[!dfv2$variant == "T183N",]

# Calculate SD and mean of each variant
dfv2_summary <- dfv2 %>%
  group_by(variant) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_gatefreq = mean(gatefreq),
    sd_gatefreq = sd(gatefreq)
  )

# Define a function to calculate the t-statistic and p-value
t_test_manual <- function(mean1, sd1, n1, mean2, sd2, n2) {
  # Calculate the t-statistic
  t_stat <- (mean1 - mean2) / sqrt((sd1^2 / n1) + (sd2^2 / n2))
  
  # Calculate the degrees of freedom
  df <- ((sd1^2 / n1) + (sd2^2 / n2))^2 / ((sd1^2 / n1)^2 / (n1 - 1) + (sd2^2 / n2)^2 / (n2 - 1))
  
  # Calculate the p-value
  p_value <- 2 * pt(abs(t_stat), df = df, lower.tail = FALSE)
  
  return(list(t_stat = t_stat, p_value = p_value))
}

# Create a file to write the output
output_file <- file("output.csv", "w")

# Perform the t-test manually
for (i in 1:nrow(dfv2_summary)) {
  if (dfv2_summary$variant[i]!= "WT" && dfv2_summary$variant[i]!= "empty_vector") {
    # Perform the t-test manually between WT and current row
    wt_result <- t_test_manual(dfv2_summary$mean_gatefreq[dfv2_summary$variant == "WT"], 
                               dfv2_summary$sd_gatefreq[dfv2_summary$variant == "WT"], 
                               3, 
                               dfv2_summary$mean_gatefreq[i], 
                               dfv2_summary$sd_gatefreq[i], 
                               3)
    
    # Perform the t-test manually between empty_vector and current row
    ev_result <- t_test_manual(dfv2_summary$mean_gatefreq[dfv2_summary$variant == "empty_vector"], 
                               dfv2_summary$sd_gatefreq[dfv2_summary$variant == "empty_vector"], 
                               3, 
                               dfv2_summary$mean_gatefreq[i], 
                               dfv2_summary$sd_gatefreq[i], 
                               3)
    
    # Multiply the p-value by 2 to bonferroni correct for 2 tests per variant
    wt_result$p_value <- wt_result$p_value * 2
    ev_result$p_value <- ev_result$p_value * 2
    
    # Write the results to the output file
    writeLines(c(paste("Results for", dfv2_summary$variant[i]), 
                 paste("WT: t-statistic =", wt_result$t_stat, ", p-value =", wt_result$p_value), 
                 paste("empty_vector: t-statistic =", ev_result$t_stat, ", p-value =", ev_result$p_value), 
                 ""), output_file)
  }
}

# Perform the t-test manually between empty_vector and WT
ev_wt_result <- t_test_manual(dfv2_summary$mean_gatefreq[dfv2_summary$variant == "empty_vector"], 
                              dfv2_summary$sd_gatefreq[dfv2_summary$variant == "empty_vector"], 
                              3, 
                              dfv2_summary$mean_gatefreq[dfv2_summary$variant == "WT"], 
                              dfv2_summary$sd_gatefreq[dfv2_summary$variant == "WT"], 
                              3)

# Multiply the p-value by 2 to bonferroni correct for 2 tests per variant
ev_wt_result$p_value <- ev_wt_result$p_value * 2

# Write the results to the output file
writeLines(c(paste("Results for empty_vector vs WT"), 
             paste("t-statistic =", ev_wt_result$t_stat, ", p-value =", ev_wt_result$p_value), 
             ""), output_file)

# Close the output file
close(output_file)

dfv2_summary$variant <- factor(dfv2_summary$variant, levels = c('empty_vector','WT', 'W78R','R92W', 'S278R', 'T183N', 'P400L', 'A403T', 'D503N'))
dfv2_summary$type <- ifelse(dfv2_summary$variant %in% c('empty_vector'), 'empty_vector', 
                             ifelse(dfv2_summary$variant == 'WT', 'WT', 
                                    ifelse(dfv2_summary$variant %in% c('R92W', 'W78R'), 'pathogenic', 'benign')))

# Create the plot
p <- ggplot(dfv2_summary, aes(x = variant, y = mean_gatefreq, fill = type)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) + 
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_gatefreq - sd_gatefreq, ymax = mean_gatefreq + sd_gatefreq), width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) + 
  labs(x = "variant", y = "gate frequency") + 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("empty_vector" = "gray", "WT" = "black", "pathogenic" = "magenta", "benign" = "darkgreen")) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)

ggsave(filename = "preliminary_barplot.pdf", p, width = 8, height = 5)
print(p)
```
```{r nonsense_and_synonymous_AIRE_variant_distribution}
#this chunk generates nonsense and synonymous distributions for Figure S5B and Figure S5C

#load map
scores <- read.csv("20231018_WholeMap.csv", header=TRUE)
#subset only synon and nonsense
scores_non_synon <- scores %>%
  filter(grepl("=", hgvs_pro) | grepl("Ter", hgvs_pro))
#draw histogram
AIRE_non_synon <- ggplot(scores_non_synon, aes(x = score, fill = ifelse(grepl("=", hgvs_pro), "Synonymous", "Nonsense"))) + 
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05, alpha = 0.5, position = "identity", color = NA) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("magenta", "green"), name = "") + 
  labs(x= "map functional score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
#print to console
print(AIRE_non_synon)
#save plots
ggsave(AIRE_non_synon, device = "pdf", filename = "AIRE_dist_non_synon.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)

#remove nonsense in last 8 amino acids since poorly detected
#extract the position
scores_non_synon$position <- as.numeric(sub("p\\.\\D+(\\d+).*", "\\1", scores_non_synon$hgvs_pro))
#filter out Ter variants from position 537 onward
scores_non_synon_filtered <- subset(scores_non_synon, !(grepl("Ter", hgvs_pro) & position >= 537))

#draw histogram
AIRE_non_synon_nolast8non <- ggplot(scores_non_synon_filtered, aes(x = score, fill = ifelse(grepl("=", hgvs_pro), "Synonymous", "Nonsense"))) + 
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 0.05, alpha = 0.5, position = "identity", color = NA) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("magenta", "green"), name = "") + 
  labs(x= "map functional score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
#print to console
print(AIRE_non_synon_nolast8non)
#save plots
ggsave(AIRE_non_synon_nolast8non, device = "pdf", filename = "AIRE_dist_non_synon_nolast8aa.pdf", width = 6, height = 5)

#how many scores >1?
scores_above_1 <- scores[scores$score>1,]

# Wilcoxon analysis (one-sided: nonsense < synonymous)
# First dataset: all nonsense + synonymous
category1_data <- subset(scores_non_synon, grepl("=", hgvs_pro))   # Synonymous
category2_data <- subset(scores_non_synon, grepl("Ter", hgvs_pro)) # Nonsense

test_result_all <- wilcox.test(category2_data$score, category1_data$score,
                               mu = 0, alt = "two.sided", conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.95,
                               paired = FALSE, correct = TRUE)
print(test_result_all)

# Second dataset: filtered (no nonsense in last 8 amino acids)
category1_data_filt <- subset(scores_non_synon_filtered, grepl("=", hgvs_pro))   # Synonymous
category2_data_filt <- subset(scores_non_synon_filtered, grepl("Ter", hgvs_pro)) # Nonsense

test_result_filt <- wilcox.test(category2_data_filt$score, category1_data_filt$score,
                                mu = 0, alt = "two.sided", conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.95,
                                paired = FALSE, correct = TRUE)
print(test_result_filt)

#summarize medians
median_summary <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("All", "Filtered_noLast8"),
  Synonymous = c(
    median(category1_data$score, na.rm = TRUE),
    median(category1_data_filt$score, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  Nonsense = c(
    median(category2_data$score, na.rm = TRUE),
    median(category2_data_filt$score, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)

print(median_summary)
```

```{r biological_replicate_comparison, fig.show="asis", out.width="50%", echo=FALSE}
#this chunk generates biological replicate correlation for Figure S5A and describes the command line script needed to draw the map in Figure 1B

#read in redos of region 1
rep1_Reg1 <- read.csv("Rep1Redo_modal_Rep1_select_t1_simple_aa.csv", comment.char = "#")
rep2_Reg1 <- read.csv("modal_Rep2_select_t1_simple_aa.csv", comment.char = "#")

#read in rest of map
#need to eliminate region 1 from these scores - region 1 was re-sequenced, and needs to be replaced
rep1_other <- read.csv("rep1_modal_binfour_t1_simple_aa.csv", comment.char = "#")
rep2_other <- read.csv("rep2_modal_binfour_t1_simple_aa.csv", comment.char = "#")

# Extract position using regular expression
pos_extract <- function(data) {
regexp <- "[[:digit:]]+"
data$pos <- str_extract(data$hgvs_pro, regexp)
data$pos <- as.numeric(data$pos)
return(data)
}

rep1_Reg1 <- pos_extract(rep1_Reg1)
rep2_Reg1 <- pos_extract(rep2_Reg1)
rep1_other <- pos_extract(rep1_other)
rep2_other <- pos_extract(rep2_other)

#find max position in reg 1
max(rep1_Reg1$pos)
max(rep2_Reg1$pos)

#remove region 1 from reps
rep1_other <- rep1_other[rep1_other$pos > 182,]
rep2_other <- rep2_other[rep2_other$pos > 182,]

colnames(rep1_Reg1)[colnames(rep1_Reg1) == "score"] <- "rep1"
colnames(rep2_Reg1)[colnames(rep2_Reg1) == "score"] <- "rep2"
colnames(rep1_other)[colnames(rep1_other) == "score"] <- "rep1"
colnames(rep2_other)[colnames(rep2_other) == "score"] <- "rep2"

merge_Reg1 <- merge(rep1_Reg1, rep2_Reg1, by = "hgvs_pro")
merge_rest <- merge(rep1_other, rep2_other, by = "hgvs_pro")

#for further analysis, save map replicates 
Rep1_inFull <- rbind(rep1_Reg1, rep1_other)
Rep2_inFull <- rbind(rep2_Reg1, rep2_other)
colnames(Rep1_inFull)[colnames(Rep1_inFull) == "rep1"] <- "score"
colnames(Rep2_inFull)[colnames(Rep2_inFull) == "rep2"] <- "score"
write.csv(file = "Rep1_AIRE_forMap.csv", Rep1_inFull, row.names=FALSE)
write.csv(file = "Rep2_AIRE_forMap.csv", Rep2_inFull, row.names=FALSE)
#have a set that only includes variants mentioned in both replicates
shared_variants <- intersect(Rep1_inFull$hgvs_pro, Rep2_inFull$hgvs_pro)
#subset each replicate to only include shared variants
Rep1_common <- Rep1_inFull[Rep1_inFull$hgvs_pro %in% shared_variants, ]
Rep2_common <- Rep2_inFull[Rep2_inFull$hgvs_pro %in% shared_variants, ]
write.csv(file = "Rep1_AIRE_forMap_onlyinBothReps.csv", Rep1_common, row.names=FALSE)
write.csv(file = "Rep2_AIRE_forMap_onlyinBothReps.csv", Rep2_common, row.names=FALSE)
#extract uncommon variants and make separate set that will be read later for the final MaveDB table
Rep1_uncommon <- Rep1_inFull[!(Rep1_inFull$hgvs_pro %in% shared_variants), ]
Rep2_uncommon <- Rep2_inFull[!(Rep2_inFull$hgvs_pro %in% shared_variants), ]
#merge uncommon and save to be added later
UncommonVariants_Merged_bothReps <- rbind(Rep1_uncommon, Rep2_uncommon)
table(duplicated(Rep1_common$hgvs_pro))
table(duplicated(Rep2_common$hgvs_pro))
table(duplicated(UncommonVariants_Merged_bothReps$hgvs_pro))
write.csv(UncommonVariants_Merged_bothReps, "uncommonReps.csv", row.names = FALSE)
#mergemaps, test using non-intersected version for map drawing, then intersected version for the set that will be used for clinical variants
#tsm mergeMaps --maps Rep1_AIRE_forMap.csv Rep2_AIRE_forMap.csv
#output is called: Both_Reps_nonIntersect_merged_map.csv
#tsm mergeMaps --maps Rep1_AIRE_forMap_onlyinBothReps.csv Rep2_AIRE_forMap_onlyinBothReps.csv
#output is called: Rep_Intersection_merged_map.csv

#these are the scripts used to draw the map. the one with both reps included, even if only seen once, is what was used in the manuscript
#tsm mavevisLocal -i '/Users/anna/Desktop/AIRE_Manuscript/Both_Reps_nonIntersect_merged_map.csv' -p /Users/anna/Desktop/AIRE_Manuscript/AIRE_All_Rep1_modal.json
#tsm mavevisLocal -i '/Users/anna/Desktop/AIRE_Manuscript/Rep_Intersection_merged_map.csv' -p /Users/anna/Desktop/AIRE_Manuscript/AIRE_All_Rep1_modal.json

all <- rbind(merge_Reg1, merge_rest)
#filter for -2
all <- all[all$rep1>-2&all$rep2>-2,]

#extract only missense variants
all_missense <- all[!(grepl("Ter", all$hgvs_pro)|grepl("=", all$hgvs_pro)),]

# Calculate the correlation and p-value
cor_test <- cor.test(all$rep1, all$rep2, method = "spearman")
cor_test

# Extract the correlation and p-value
correlation <- cor_test$estimate
p_value <- cor_test$p.value

# Create the biological replicate plot
p <- ggplot(all, aes(x=rep1, y=rep2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.05) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  labs(x="replicate 1 AIRE functional variant score", y="replicate 2 AIRE functional variant score")

ggsave(filename = "biorepCompare_all.pdf", p, width = 4, height = 5)
print(p)
###################missense####################
# Calculate the correlation and p-value
cor_test_missense <- cor.test(all_missense$rep1, all_missense$rep2)
cor_test_missense

# Extract the correlation and p-value
correlation <- cor_test_missense$estimate
p_value <- cor_test_missense$p.value

# Create the ggplot
p_missense <- ggplot(all_missense, aes(x=rep1, y=rep2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.05) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  labs(x="replicate 1 AIRE functional variant score", y="replicate 2 AIRE functional variant score")

ggsave(filename = "biorepCompare_missense.pdf", p_missense, width = 4, height = 5)
print(p_missense)
```

```{r process_map, fig.show="asis", out.width="50%", echo=FALSE}
#this chunk is an analysis script to process map scores to missense and add from and to amino acid columns
map <- scores
#map <- map[map$score>-4,]

process_map <- function(map) {
  # Extract missense mutations
  map_missense <- map[!(grepl("=", map$hgvs_pro) | grepl("Ter", map$hgvs_pro)),]
  
  # Extract 'from' column
  map_missense$from <- substr(map_missense$hgvs_pro, 3, 5)
  
  # Extract position using regular expression
  regexp <- "[[:digit:]]+"
  map_missense$pos <- str_extract(map_missense$hgvs_pro, regexp)
  
  # Annotate by amino acid type
map_missense <- map_missense %>% mutate(aa_type_From = case_when(
    from %in% c("Ala", "Gly", "Val", "Leu", "Ile", "Met", "Phe", "Trp", "Tyr") ~ "hydrophobic",
    from %in% c("Ser", "Thr", "Asn", "Gln", "Cys") ~ "polar",
    from %in% c("Asp", "Glu") ~ "neg_charged",
    from %in% c("Arg", "Lys", "His") ~ "pos_charged",
    from %in% c("Pro") ~ "proline",
    TRUE ~ "unknown"
  ))

  # Add column for 'change To'
  map_missense$changeTo <- substr(map_missense$hgvs_pro, nchar(map_missense$hgvs_pro) - 2, nchar(map_missense$hgvs_pro))
  
  # Annotate by amino acid type for 'change To'
  map_missense <- map_missense %>%
    mutate(aa_type_To = case_when(
      changeTo %in% c("Ala", "Gly", "Val", "Leu", "Ile", "Met", "Phe", "Trp", "Tyr") ~ "hydrophobic",
      changeTo %in% c("Ser", "Thr", "Asn", "Gln", "Cys") ~ "polar",
      changeTo %in% c("Asp", "Glu") ~ "neg_charged",
      changeTo %in% c("Arg", "Lys", "His") ~ "pos_charged",
      changeTo %in% c("Pro") ~ "proline",
      TRUE ~ "unknown"
    ))
  
  return(map_missense)
}

#Process map
map_processed <- process_map(map)
```

```{r from_and_to_simple_functions_and_run, fig.show="asis", out.width="50%", echo=FALSE}
#This chunk generates Figure S8

##############Base function: draw from which to which?############
# Function to perform plot, statistical testing, and result exporting
##########from each amino acid type instead############
#give aa categories classifications
assign_aa_category <- function(aa_vector) {
  dplyr::case_when(
    aa_vector %in% c("Ala", "Gly", "Val", "Leu", "Ile", "Met", "Phe", "Trp", "Tyr") ~ "hydrophobic",
    aa_vector %in% c("Ser", "Thr", "Asn", "Gln", "Cys") ~ "polar",
    aa_vector %in% c("Asp", "Glu") ~ "neg_charged",
    aa_vector %in% c("Arg", "Lys", "His") ~ "pos_charged",
    aa_vector %in% c("Pro") ~ "proline",
    TRUE ~ "other"
  )
}
#order the amino acids by category
get_ordered_aa_levels <- function() {
  c(
    "Ala", "Gly", "Val", "Leu", "Ile", "Met", "Phe", "Trp", "Tyr",    # hydrophobic
    "Ser", "Thr", "Asn", "Gln", "Cys",                                # polar
    "Asp", "Glu",                                                     # neg_charged
    "Arg", "Lys", "His",                                              # pos_charged
    "Pro"                                                             # proline
  )
}

analyze_data_FROM_AA <- function(data, title, width, height) {
  #arrange the categories
  aa_levels <- get_ordered_aa_levels()
  data$from <- factor(data$from, levels = aa_levels)
  data$change_to <- factor(data$changeTo, levels = aa_levels)
  #give class to categories
  data$category_from <- assign_aa_category(data$from)
  data$category_to <- assign_aa_category(data$changeTo)

  #eliminate conserved-type substitutions (e.g. polar to polar)
  data <- data[!(data$category_from == data$category_to),]
  
p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = from, y = score, fill = category_from)) + 
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size=0.5) +
  labs(title = title, x = "from amino acid", y = "functional score", fill = "category_from") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 17) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "hydrophobic" = "#4477AA",
    "polar" = "#EE6677",
    "neg_charged" = "#228833",
    "pos_charged" = "#CCBB44",
    "proline" = "#66CCEE",
    "other" = "AA3377"
  ))
print(p)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(title, ".pdf"), width = width, height = height)
  # Get all unique categories
  categories <- unique(data$from)
  
  # Initialize an empty dataframe to store the results
  results <- data.frame(Category1 = character(), Category2 = character(), 
                        p.value = numeric(), estimate = numeric(), 
                        Median1 = numeric(), Median2 = numeric())
  
  # Loop through each combination of two categories
  for(i in 1:(length(categories)-1)){
    for(j in (i+1):length(categories)){
      category1 <- categories[i]
      category2 <- categories[j]
      
      # Subset the data for the two categories
      category1_data <- data[data$from == category1,]
      category2_data <- data[data$from == category2,]
      
      # Perform the Wilcoxon test
      test_result <- wilcox.test(category1_data$score, category2_data$score, 
                                 mu=0, alt="two.sided", conf.int=T, conf.level = 0.95, 
                                 paired=F, correct=T)
      
      # Extract the p-value and estimate
      p_value <- test_result$p.value
      estimate <- test_result$estimate
      
      # Calculate medians
      median1 <- median(category1_data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
      median2 <- median(category2_data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      # Add the results to the dataframe
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Category1 = category1, Category2 = category2, 
                                           p.value = p_value, estimate = estimate, 
                                           Median1 = median1, Median2 = median2))
    }
  }
  
  # Get the number of tests performed
  n_tests <- nrow(results)
  # Apply the Bonferroni correction
  results$p.value <- p.adjust(results$p.value, method = "bonferroni")
  # Output only significant results
  sig_results <- results[results$p.value < 0.05,]
  # Write significant results to file
  write.csv(file = paste0(title, ".csv"), sig_results, row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Print the medians
  cat("Medians of categories:\n")
  for(category in categories) {
    median_value <- median(data[data$from == category,]$score, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("Category:", category, "Median:", median_value, "\n")
  }
}

##########TO each amino acid instead############
analyze_data_TO_AA <- function(data, title, width, height) {
  #arrange the categories
  aa_levels <- get_ordered_aa_levels()
  data$from <- factor(data$from, levels = aa_levels)
  data$change_to <- factor(data$changeTo, levels = aa_levels)
  #give class to categories
  data$category_from <- assign_aa_category(data$from)
  data$category_to <- assign_aa_category(data$changeTo)

  #eliminate conserved-type substitutions (e.g. polar to polar)
  data <- data[!(data$category_from == data$category_to),]
  
p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = change_to, y = score, fill = category_to)) + 
  geom_boxplot(outlier.size=0.5) +
  labs(title = title, x = "from amino acid", y = "functional score", fill = "category_to") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 17) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "hydrophobic" = "#4477AA",
    "polar" = "#EE6677",
    "neg_charged" = "#228833",
    "pos_charged" = "#CCBB44",
    "proline" = "#66CCEE",
    "other" = "AA3377"
  ))
print(p)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0(title, ".pdf"), width = width, height = height)
  # Get all unique categories
  categories <- unique(data$change_to)
  
  # Initialize an empty dataframe to store the results
  results <- data.frame(Category1 = character(), Category2 = character(), 
                        p.value = numeric(), estimate = numeric(), 
                        Median1 = numeric(), Median2 = numeric())
  
  # Loop through each combination of two categories
  for(i in 1:(length(categories)-1)){
    for(j in (i+1):length(categories)){
      category1 <- categories[i]
      category2 <- categories[j]
      
      # Subset the data for the two categories
      category1_data <- data[data$change_to == category1,]
      category2_data <- data[data$change_to == category2,]
      
      # Perform the Wilcoxon test
      test_result <- wilcox.test(category1_data$score, category2_data$score, 
                                 mu=0, alt="two.sided", conf.int=T, conf.level = 0.95, 
                                 paired=F, correct=T)
      
      # Extract the p-value and estimate
      p_value <- test_result$p.value
      estimate <- test_result$estimate
      
      # Calculate medians
      median1 <- median(category1_data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
      median2 <- median(category2_data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      # Add the results to the dataframe
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Category1 = category1, Category2 = category2, 
                                           p.value = p_value, estimate = estimate, 
                                           Median1 = median1, Median2 = median2))
    }
  }
  
  # Get the number of tests performed
  n_tests <- nrow(results)
  # Apply the Bonferroni correction
  results$p.value <- p.adjust(results$p.value, method = "bonferroni")
  # Output only significant results
  sig_results <- results[results$p.value < 0.05,]
  # Write significant results to file
  write.csv(file = paste0(title, ".csv"), sig_results, row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Print the medians
  cat("Medians of categories:\n")
  for(category in categories) {
    median_value <- median(data[data$change_to == category,]$score, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("Category:", category, "Median:", median_value, "\n")
  }
}

####Run these functions####
analyze_data_FROM_AA(map_processed, "Map Score FROM Amino Acid", width = 10, height = 6)
analyze_data_TO_AA(map_processed, "Map Score TO Amino Acid", width = 10, height = 6)

#count frequency of every amino acid of AIRE
AIRE <- ""

#split amino acid code to amino acids
amino_acids <- strsplit(AIRE, "")[[1]]
#count each amino acid
aa_counts <- table(amino_acids)
#print counts
aa_counts <- aa_counts[order(names(aa_counts))]
print(aa_counts)

```

```{r FUNC_process_map}
#this chunk runs a function to process map to change variant format and only keep missense variants

process_map <- function(map) {
  # Extract missense mutations
  map_missense <- map[!(grepl("=", map$hgvs_pro) | grepl("Ter", map$hgvs_pro)),]
  
  # Extract 'from' column
  map_missense$from <- substr(map_missense$hgvs_pro, 3, 5)
  
  # Extract position using regular expression
  regexp <- "[[:digit:]]+"
  map_missense$pos <- str_extract(map_missense$hgvs_pro, regexp)
  
  # Annotate by amino acid type
map_missense <- map_missense %>% mutate(aa_type_From = case_when(
    from %in% c("Ala", "Gly", "Val", "Leu", "Ile", "Met", "Phe", "Trp", "Tyr") ~ "hydrophobic",
    from %in% c("Ser", "Thr", "Asn", "Gln", "Cys") ~ "polar",
    from %in% c("Asp", "Glu") ~ "neg_charged",
    from %in% c("Arg", "Lys", "His") ~ "pos_charged",
    from %in% c("Pro") ~ "proline",
    TRUE ~ "unknown"
  ))

  # Add column for 'change To'
  map_missense$changeTo <- substr(map_missense$hgvs_pro, nchar(map_missense$hgvs_pro) - 2, nchar(map_missense$hgvs_pro))
  
  # Annotate by amino acid type for 'change To'
  map_missense <- map_missense %>%
    mutate(aa_type_To = case_when(
      changeTo %in% c("Ala", "Gly", "Val", "Leu", "Ile", "Met", "Phe", "Trp", "Tyr") ~ "hydrophobic",
      changeTo %in% c("Ser", "Thr", "Asn", "Gln", "Cys") ~ "polar",
      changeTo %in% c("Asp", "Glu") ~ "neg_charged",
      changeTo %in% c("Arg", "Lys", "His") ~ "pos_charged",
      changeTo %in% c("Pro") ~ "proline",
      TRUE ~ "unknown"
    ))
  
  return(map_missense)
}

map_processed <- process_map(map)
```

```{r FUNC_analyze_data_FROM}
#this chunk generates functions for amino acid substitutions from and to amino acid types, but isn't used directly as a figure

analyze_data_FROM <- function(data, title, width, height) {
  #remove conservatives
  data <- data[data$aa_type_To != data$aa_type_From,]
  # Create the initial plot
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = aa_type_From, y = score)) + 
    geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3) +
    labs(x = "from amino acid type", y = "variant functional score") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 17)
  
  # Get all unique categories
  categories <- unique(data$aa_type_From)
  
  # Initialize an empty dataframe to store the results
  results <- data.frame(Category1 = character(), Category2 = character(), 
                        p.value = numeric(), estimate = numeric(), 
                        Median1 = numeric(), Median2 = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # List to store comparisons for stat_compare_means
  comparisons <- list()
  
  # Loop through each combination of two categories
  for(i in 1:(length(categories)-1)){
    for(j in (i+1):length(categories)){
      category1 <- categories[i]
      category2 <- categories[j]
      
      # Subset the data for the two categories
      category1_data <- data[data$aa_type_From == category1,]
      category2_data <- data[data$aa_type_From == category2,]
      
      # Perform the Wilcoxon test
      test_result <- wilcox.test(category1_data$score, category2_data$score, 
                                 mu=0, alt="two.sided", conf.int=T, conf.level = 0.95, 
                                 paired=F, correct=T)
      
      # Extract the p-value and estimate
      p_value <- test_result$p.value
      estimate <- test_result$estimate
      
      # Calculate medians
      median1 <- median(category1_data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
      median2 <- median(category2_data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      # Add the results to the dataframe
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Category1 = category1, Category2 = category2, 
                                           p.value = p_value, estimate = estimate, 
                                           Median1 = median1, Median2 = median2))
    }
  }
  
  # Apply the Bonferroni correction
  results$p.value <- p.adjust(results$p.value, method = "bonferroni")
  
  # Filter only significant results
  sig_results <- results[results$p.value < 0.05,]
  
  # Write significant results to file
  write.csv(file = paste0(title, ".csv"), sig_results, row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Add significance bars to the plot for significant results
  if (nrow(sig_results) > 0) {
    sig_comparisons <- mapply(function(cat1, cat2) c(cat1, cat2), 
                              sig_results$Category1, sig_results$Category2, 
                              SIMPLIFY = FALSE)
    p <- p + stat_compare_means(comparisons = sig_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", 
                                label = "p.signif", p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
  }
  
  # Print the plot to PDF
  ggsave(p, device = "pdf", filename = paste0(title, ".pdf"), width = width, height = height)

  # Print the plot to the console
  print(p)
  
  # Print the medians
  cat("Medians of categories:\n")
  for(category in categories) {
    median_value <- median(data[data$aa_type_From == category,]$score, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("Category:", category, "Median:", median_value, "\n")
  }
}

analyze_data_FROM(map_processed, "missense score FROM amino acid type", width = 6, height = 6)
```
```{r FUNC_analyze_data_TO_simple, echo = FALSE, fig.show="hold", out.width="50%"}
#This chunk generates Figure S9
#to amino acid simplified
analyze_data_TO_simple <- function(data, To_category, pdf_filename, csv_filename) {
  df_name <- deparse(substitute(data))
  
  # Remove conservatives (change from same category to same category)
  data <- data[data$aa_type_To != data$aa_type_From,]
  
  # Create a simple category variable
  data <- data %>%
    mutate(aa_type_To_Simple = ifelse(aa_type_To == To_category, To_category, paste("not", To_category, sep = " ")))

  # Set the order of the factor levels for boxplot
  data$aa_type_To_Simple <- factor(data$aa_type_To_Simple, levels = c(To_category, paste("not", To_category, sep = " ")))

  # Create the boxplot
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = aa_type_To_Simple, y = score)) + 
    geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3) + 
    labs(title = df_name, x = "to amino acid category", y = "variant functional score") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 17)
  
  # Get unique categories for comparisons
  categories <- levels(data$aa_type_To_Simple)  # Use levels to ensure the correct order
  
  # Initialize results dataframe
  results <- data.frame(Category1 = character(), Category2 = character(), p.value = numeric(), estimate = numeric())
  comparisons <- list()
  
  # Perform pairwise comparisons
  for(i in 1:(length(categories) - 1)){
    for(j in (i + 1):length(categories)){
      category1 <- categories[i]
      category2 <- categories[j]
      
      category1_data <- data %>% filter(aa_type_To_Simple == category1)
      category2_data <- data %>% filter(aa_type_To_Simple == category2)
      
      test_result <- wilcox.test(category1_data$score, category2_data$score, 
                                 mu = 0, alt = "two.sided", conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.95, 
                                 paired = FALSE, correct = TRUE)
      
      p_value <- test_result$p.value
      estimate <- test_result$estimate
      
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Category1 = category1, Category2 = category2, p.value = p_value, estimate = estimate))
      comparisons <- append(comparisons, list(c(category1, category2)))
    }
  }
  
  # Apply Bonferroni correction
  results$p.value <- p.adjust(results$p.value, method = "bonferroni")
  
  # Print results
  print(results)
  
  # Filter significant results
  sig_results <- results[results$p.value < 0.05,]
  
  # Write significant results to CSV
  write.csv(sig_results, file = csv_filename, row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Print medians of categories
  cat("Medians of categories:\n")
  for(category in categories) {
    median_value <- median(data[data$aa_type_To_Simple == category,]$score, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("Category:", category, "Median:", median_value, "\n")
  }
  
  # Add significance bars if there are significant results
  if (nrow(sig_results) > 0) {
    sig_comparisons <- comparisons[results$p.value < 0.05]
    p <- p + stat_compare_means(comparisons = sig_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", 
                                label = "p.signif", p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
  }
  
  # Save plot
  ggsave(p, device = "pdf", filename = pdf_filename, width = 3, height = 6)
  print(p)
}

analyze_data_TO_simple(map_processed, "proline", "to_proline.pdf", "to_proline.csv")
analyze_data_TO_simple(map_processed, "hydrophobic", "to_hydrophobic.pdf", "to_hydrophobic.csv")
analyze_data_TO_simple(map_processed, "neg_charged", "to_neg_charged.pdf", "to_neg_charged.csv")
analyze_data_TO_simple(map_processed, "pos_charged", "to_pos_charged.pdf", "to_pos_charged.csv")
```
```{r PLOT_from_categoryToOthers, fig.show="hold", out.width="50%", echo = FALSE}
#This chunk generates Figure S8
#calculate the median score and count for each category change
median_scores <- map_processed %>%
  group_by(aa_type_From, aa_type_To) %>%
  summarise(
    median_score = median(score, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(aa_type_From, aa_type_To)

#save median results
print(median_scores)
write.csv(median_scores, "medianScoresFromTocategories.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#get unique 'from' types
from_types <- unique(map_processed$aa_type_From)

#extract all positions that are initially one or the other to see where they are distributed in the protein
positive_charge_pos <- sort(as.numeric(unique(map_processed[map_processed$aa_type_From=="pos_charged",]$pos)))
negative_charge_pos <- sort(as.numeric(unique(map_processed[map_processed$aa_type_From=="neg_charged",]$pos)))
hydrophobic_pos_all <- sort(as.numeric(unique(map_processed[map_processed$aa_type_From=="hydrophobic",]$pos)))
polar_pos <- sort(as.numeric(unique(map_processed[map_processed$aa_type_From=="polar",]$pos)))
proline_pos <- sort(as.numeric(unique(map_processed[map_processed$aa_type_From=="proline",]$pos)))

#save to paint onto structure
write.csv(positive_charge_pos, "positive_charge_positions.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(negative_charge_pos, "negative_charge_positions.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(hydrophobic_pos_all, "hydrophobic_positions.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(polar_pos, "polar_positions.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(proline_pos, "proline_positions.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#make this into a correlation-matrix-type plot
#reformat data to be compatible with plot
correlation_matrix <- median_scores %>%
  select(aa_type_From, aa_type_To, median_score) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = aa_type_To,
    values_from = median_score
  )
print(correlation_matrix)

#reorder types so "proline" comes last
types <- setdiff(sort(unique(c(median_scores$aa_type_From, median_scores$aa_type_To))), "proline")
types <- c("proline", types)

#expand to full grid and pivot
correlation_matrix_full <- expand.grid(
  aa_type_From = types,
  aa_type_To = types
) %>%
  left_join(median_scores, by = c("aa_type_From", "aa_type_To")) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = aa_type_To, values_from = median_score)
print(correlation_matrix_full)

heatmap_data <- expand.grid(
  aa_type_From = types,
  aa_type_To = types
) %>%
  left_join(median_scores, by = c("aa_type_From", "aa_type_To")) %>%
  mutate(
    aa_type_From = factor(aa_type_From, levels = types),
    aa_type_To = factor(aa_type_To, levels = types)
  )

#Plot heatmap
p <- ggplot(heatmap_data, aes(x = aa_type_To, y = aa_type_From, fill = median_score)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(median_score, 2)), na.rm = TRUE, size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "skyblue", high = "darkred", na.value = "grey90") +
  coord_fixed() +  # force square tiles
  labs(
    x = "TO",
    y = "From",
    fill = "Median Score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y.right = element_text()  # if using right-side y labels later
  )

print(p)
ggsave("corrMatrix.pdf", p, device = "pdf", width = 5, height = 5)
```


```{r positional_function}
#This chunk calculates amino acid positional map scores
process_positional <- function(file_path) {
  # Read the data
  data_table <- fread(file_path)
  #only include missense variants
  data_table <- data_table[!(grepl("=", data_table$hgvs_pro)|grepl("Ter", data_table$hgvs_pro)),]
  # Extract amino acid information
  data_table[, from_aa := str_extract_all(hgvs_pro, "\\w+\\d+")]
  data_table[, from_aa := str_extract(hgvs_pro, "\\d+")]
  # Calculate positional mean scores
  positional_mean_scores <- data_table[, .(score = sum(score, na.rm = TRUE) / .N), by = "from_aa"]
  # Convert 'from_aa' to numeric
  positional_mean_scores$from_aa <- as.numeric(positional_mean_scores$from_aa)
  return(positional_mean_scores)
}

#find average score by position 
map_positional <- process_positional("20231018_WholeMap.csv")
write.csv(file = "map_positional.csv", map_positional, row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r choose_fasa_cutoffs, echo = FALSE}
#This chunk generates Figure S6
#process sasa scores
sasa_scores_full <-read.table("AIRE_SASA_Output.txt")
freesasa_data <- data.frame(sasa_scores_full$V3, sasa_scores_full$V6)
names(freesasa_data)[names(freesasa_data) == "sasa_scores_full.V6"] <- "sasa"
names(freesasa_data)[names(freesasa_data) == "sasa_scores_full.V3"] <- "from_aa"

#annotated with buried or not
freesasa_data$location <- case_when(
  freesasa_data$sasa <= 50  ~ "buried",
  freesasa_data$sasa >= 110 ~ "surface",
  TRUE                      ~ NA
)

write.csv(file = "freeSASAannot.csv", freesasa_data, row.names = FALSE)

p <- ggplot(freesasa_data, aes(x = sasa)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, fill = "grey", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 50, color = "magenta", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 110, color = "green", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "freeSASA", x = "SASA (Å²)", y = "Frequency") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)

print(p)

#plot histogram and draw lines that define core or surface based on sasa score
ggsave(p, filename = "allatoms_rel_freesasa.pdf", width = 8, height = 5, device = "pdf")
```

```{r plot_score_by_protein_position, fig.show="hold", out.width="50%", echo = FALSE}
#This chunk analyzes surface and core positional scores, using freeSASA daa processed above. This chunk generates Figure 2A and Figure S10B.
#filter by core and surface values
filtered_sasa50 <- freesasa_data %>% 
  filter(sasa < 50) %>%
  mutate(from_aa = as.numeric(from_aa))

filtered_sasa110 <- freesasa_data %>% 
  filter(sasa > 110) %>%
  mutate(from_aa = as.numeric(from_aa))

#function to process SASA scores and generate significance files
process_SASA_scores <- function(scores_data, freesasa_data) {
  
  #make core/buried amino acid category
  core_scores <- merge(scores_data, filtered_sasa50, by = "from_aa") %>% 
    select(from_aa, score)
  write.csv(core_scores, paste0("AIRE_", "Core_freesasa.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  #make exposed amino acid category
  surface_scores <- merge(scores_data, filtered_sasa110, by = "from_aa") %>% 
    select(from_aa, score)
  write.csv(surface_scores, paste0("AIRE_", "Surface_freesasa.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
  #combine core and surface scores
  combined_scores <- merge(core_scores, surface_scores, by = "from_aa", all = TRUE)
  names(combined_scores) <- c("from_aa", "core", "surface")
  
  #write combined data to CSV
  write.csv(combined_scores, "AIRE_combined_freesasa.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  
  #combinee data for plotting
  combined_long <- combined_scores %>% 
    pivot_longer(cols = c(core, surface), names_to = "Structure_Type", values_to = "score") %>% 
    na.omit()  # Remove rows with NA values
  
  #print medians to console
  cat("Medians:\n")
  cat("Buried:", median(combined_scores$core, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  cat("Surface:", median(combined_scores$surface, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
  #print table of number of core and surface
  print(table(combined_long$Structure_Type))
  
  #create the boxplot
  p <- ggplot(combined_long, aes(x = Structure_Type, y = score)) + 
    geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) + 
    labs(x = "Amino Acid Location", y = "Functional Score") + 
    theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
  
  #perform significance tests
  sig_tests <- compare_means(score ~ Structure_Type, data = combined_long, 
                             method = "wilcox.test", p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
  
  #filter significant results
  sig_tests <- sig_tests %>% filter(p.adj < 0.05)
  
  #save significant hits to a CSV file
  if (nrow(sig_tests) > 0) {
    write.csv(sig_tests, paste0(deparse(substitute(scores_data)), "_AIRE_positional_significant_hits.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  }
  #create a list of significant comparisons
  significant_comparisons <- lapply(1:nrow(sig_tests), function(i) {
    c(sig_tests$group1[i], sig_tests$group2[i])
  })
  #add significance bars to the plot if there are significant comparisons
  if (nrow(sig_tests) > 0) {
    p <- p + stat_compare_means(comparisons = significant_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif")
  }
  print(p)
  #save plot
  ggsave(filename = paste(deparse(substitute(scores_data)), "AIRE_freesasa_boxplot.pdf", sep = "_"), height = 4, width = 3, device = "pdf")
}

#evaluate map for boxplot
process_SASA_scores(map_positional, freesasa_data)

#extract only CARD domain positions
CARD_domain <- map_positional[map_positional$from_aa < 106,]
# Evaluate only CARD domain for core and surface
process_SASA_scores(CARD_domain, freesasa_data)

#run these plots using only high-confidence AlphaFold prediction data 
#input AlphaFold confidence scores
alphafoldConf <- read.csv("AF_confidenceScores_AIRE.csv", header = FALSE)
alphafoldConf_cleaned <- alphafoldConf[-c(1:7), , drop = FALSE]

#split column into scores and header
alphafoldConf_df <- read.table(text = alphafoldConf_cleaned$V1, 
                               fill = TRUE, 
                               header = FALSE, 
                               col.names = c("label_asym_id", "label_comp_id", "label_seq_id", "metric_id", "metric_value", "model_id", "ordinal_id"))

#extract the necessary columns
alphafold_necc_info <- alphafoldConf_df[, c("label_comp_id", "label_seq_id", "metric_value")]
colnames(alphafold_necc_info) <- c("AA", "from_aa", "AF_confidence")

#merge confidence data with positional data
map_positional_w_confidence <- merge(alphafold_necc_info, map_positional, by = "from_aa")
high_confidence_map_positional <- map_positional_w_confidence[map_positional_w_confidence$AF_confidence > 70,]

#process SASA scores for high-confidence positions
process_SASA_scores(high_confidence_map_positional, freesasa_data)

#check if big hydrophobic residues are buried according to freesasa
big_hydrophobic <- map_processed[grepl("Phe|Tyr|Trp|Ile", map_processed$from),]
#extract unique hydrophobic positions
hydrophobic_pos <- unique(big_hydrophobic$pos)
#calculate the proportion of buried hydrophobic positions
buried_hydrophobic_ratio <- length(intersect(filtered_sasa50$from_aa, hydrophobic_pos)) / length(hydrophobic_pos)
exposed_hydrophobic_ratio <- length(intersect(filtered_sasa110$from_aa, hydrophobic_pos)) / length(hydrophobic_pos)
```

```{r map_scores_by_domain}
#This chunk compares scores by domain type, but does not generate figures for the mansucript
#label by domain
map_processed$pos <- as.numeric(map_processed$pos)
map_processed <- map_processed %>%
  mutate(Domain = case_when(
    pos >= 1 & pos <= 105 ~ "CARD",
    pos >= 101 & pos <= 178 ~ "Disordered",
    pos >= 181 & pos <= 233 ~ "SAND not disordered",
    pos >= 234 & pos <= 290 ~ "Disordered",
    pos >= 296 & pos <= 343 ~ "PHD1",
    pos >= 348 & pos <= 382 ~ "Disordered",
    pos >= 434 & pos <= 475 ~ "PHD2",
    pos >= 489 & pos <= 508 ~ "Disordered",
    pos >= 515 & pos <= 545 ~ "C-term",
    TRUE ~ "no known"
  ))
# Create the boxplot
mapbyDomains <- ggplot(map_processed, aes(x = Domain, y = score)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) + 
  labs(x = "domain", y = "variant functional score")

ggsave(filename = "AIRE_domains_boxplot.pdf", plot = mapbyDomains, height = 5, width = 7, device = "pdf")

# Calculate medians for each domain
medians <- map_processed %>%
  group_by(Domain) %>%
  summarise(median_score = median(score, na.rm = TRUE))

# Print medians
print(medians)

# Perform the significance tests
sig_tests <- compare_means(score ~ Domain, data = map_processed, 
                           method = "wilcox.test", p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")

# Filter significant results
sig_tests <- sig_tests %>% filter(p.adj < 0.05)

# Save the significant hits to a CSV file
if (nrow(sig_tests) > 0) {
  write.csv(sig_tests, "AIRE_domains_significant_hits.csv", row.names = FALSE)
}

# Create a list of significant comparisons
significant_comparisons <- list()
for (i in 1:nrow(sig_tests)) {
  comparison <- c(sig_tests$group1[i], sig_tests$group2[i])
  significant_comparisons <- c(significant_comparisons, list(comparison))
}

# Add significance bars to the plot if there are significant comparisons
if (nrow(sig_tests) > 0) {
  mapbyDomains <- mapbyDomains + 
    stat_compare_means(comparisons = significant_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif", hide.ns = TRUE)
}

# Print the plot with significance bars
print(mapbyDomains)
ggsave(filename = "AIRE_domains_boxplotsig.pdf", plot = mapbyDomains, height = 7, width = 5, device = "pdf")
```

```{r generate_reference_set, echo = FALSE}
#this chunk establishes reference set using clinvar reference set, no figures
#read in clinvar reference set (only missense)
clinvar <- read.csv("20250825_AIRE_missense_clinvar_result.csv")

#remove conflicting variants
clinvar <- clinvar[!grepl("Conflict", clinvar$Germline.classification),]
table(clinvar$Germline.classification)

#assign positive to pathogenic variants, negative to benign variants
clinvar_hgvsp <- clinvar %>%
  mutate(referenceSet = case_when(
    grepl("athogenic", Germline.classification) ~ "Positive",
    grepl("enign", clinvar$Germline.classification) ~ "Negative",
    TRUE ~ "no known"
  ))
#change to hgvsp format
clinvar_hgvsp$Protein.change <- mgsub(clinvar_hgvsp$Protein.change, pattern = c("A", "R", "N", "D", "C", "Q", "E", 
                                      "G", "H", "I", "L", "K", "M", "F", "P",
                                      "S", "T", "W", "Y", "V"), 
                      replacement = c("Ala", "Arg", "Asn", "Asp", "Cys", "Gln",
                                                "Glu", "Gly", "His", "Ile", "Leu", "Lys",
                                                "Met", "Phe", "Pro", "Ser",
                                                "Thr", "Trp", "Tyr", "Val"))
colnames(clinvar_hgvsp)[colnames(clinvar_hgvsp) == "Protein.change"] ="hgvsp"
clinvar_hgvsp$hgvsp <- paste("p.", clinvar_hgvsp$hgvsp, sep = "")
#remove the non-provided classifications, and the VUS
clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown <- clinvar_hgvsp[!grepl("not|significance", clinvar_hgvsp$Germline.classification),]
#confirmed no duplicates
duplicate_test <- clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown[duplicated(clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown$hgvsp),]

#create a version with only multiple submitters mentioned
clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_multipleSubmitters <- clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown[grepl("multiple", clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown$Germline.review.status),]
#create a version with only one star needed ("criteria provided")
clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar <- clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown[grepl("submitter", clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown$Germline.review.status),]

#only keep necessary columns
clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown <- clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown[,c("hgvsp","Germline.classification","referenceSet")]
clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar <- clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar[,c("hgvsp","Germline.classification","referenceSet")]
clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_multipleSubmitters <- clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_multipleSubmitters[,c("hgvsp","Germline.classification","referenceSet")]

#save reference set
write.csv(clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown, "20250825_clinvar_refset_all.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_multipleSubmitters, "20250825_clinvar_refset_multipleSubmitters.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar, "20250825_clinvar_refset_oneStar.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```
```{r extractingRevelScores, echo = FALSE}
#This chunk extracts correct variant format to provide file to ensembl VEP to extract REVEL scores, no figures

#AIRE_var_VEPformat <- paste(21, " ", REVELscoresAIRE$grch38_pos, " ", REVELscoresAIRE$grch38_pos, " ", REVELscoresAIRE$ref, "/", REVELscoresAIRE$alt, " ", 1, sep = "")
#write.table(AIRE_var_VEPformat, "VEP_submitREvel.txt", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)

#input the output from the VEP, remove the # from the beginning of the file
REVELread <- read.table("dxigbB5erc0diVQG.txt", header = TRUE)
REVELreadAIRE <- REVELread[REVELread$Gene == "ENSG00000160224",]  
REVELreadAIREmissense <- REVELread[grepl("missense_variant", REVELread$Consequence), ]
uniqueREVELreadAIREmissense <- REVELreadAIREmissense[!duplicated(REVELreadAIREmissense$Uploaded_variation), ]

#convert REVEL scores to useable scores
uniqueREVELreadAIREmissense$hgvs_pro <- paste(substr(uniqueREVELreadAIREmissense$Amino_acids, start = 1, stop = 1), uniqueREVELreadAIREmissense$Protein_position, substr(uniqueREVELreadAIREmissense$Amino_acids, start = 3, stop = 3), sep = "")
#Change variant notation to hgvs_pro
uniqueREVELreadAIREmissense$hgvs_pro <- mgsub(uniqueREVELreadAIREmissense$hgvs_pro, pattern = c("A", "R", "N", "D", "C", "Q", "E", 
                                                    "G", "H", "I", "L", "K", "M", "F", "P",
                                                    "O", "S", "U", "T", "W", "Y", "V"), 
                        replacement = c("Ala", "Arg", "Asn", "Asp", "Cys", "Gln",
                                        "Glu", "Gly", "His", "Ile", "Leu", "Lys",
                                        "Met", "Phe", "Pro", "Pyl", "Ser", "Sec",
                                        "Thr", "Trp", "Tyr", "Val"))

#Add p. to notation
uniqueREVELreadAIREmissense$hgvs_pro <- paste0("p.", uniqueREVELreadAIREmissense$hgvs_pro)
#extract columns needed
REVEL_processed <- uniqueREVELreadAIREmissense[,c("hgvs_pro", "REVEL")]
names(REVEL_processed)[names(REVEL_processed) == 'REVEL'] <- 'score'
REVEL_test <- REVEL_processed[!duplicated(REVEL_processed$hgvs_pro),]
write.csv(REVEL_test, "20260406_REVEL.csv", row.names =FALSE)
``` 

```{r process_compPredictors_andPRC_OnlyMapScoresMeasuredInBothReps, echo = FALSE} 
#This chunk processes variant effect predictor scores, and outlines how the PRCs were generated using these reference sets. 

#ESM1b: these are already *-1
ESM <- read.csv("20240122_ESM1B_maveDB.csv")
AM <- read.csv("20240122_AM_maveDB.csv")
REVEL <- read.csv("20260406_REVEL.csv")
SIFT <- read.csv("20240122_sift_maveDB.csv")
VAR <- read.csv("20240122_varity_maveDB.csv")
#use map scores only if they were measured in both replicates
map <- read.csv("Rep_Intersection_merged_map.csv")

#PRC code used for drawing plots is located here. script launched is located at bottom of this section. https://github.com/rothlab/docs/wiki/Drawing-PRC-curves

#Important note: The 'map' that goes into the script needs to consider "damaging" variants the opposite direction as the predictors. The Roth lab maps consider damaging as negative, while varity, CPT, AlphaMissense consider damaging as positive. Of the predictors used, ESM1b scores consider damaging as negative, and were multiplied by -1 prior to consideration as a predictor (the input file has already been multiplied prior to this script). 

#This script finds the number of negative and positive reference set variants for each reference set used since the PRC script outputs the initial reference set numbers. Calculate the correct intersection between the variants. The PRC script only uses the variants which have scores for all predictors and is found in the reference set, but prints the wrong number of refset variants on the output plot. This saves new reference sets that have the number of intersections.
#find how many variants intersect between all computational predictors and map with and without PHD1 domain, and with and without REVEL

refset <- clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar

  # add position column
  regexp <- "[[:digit:]]+"
  refset$pos <- as.numeric(str_extract(refset$hgvsp, regexp))

  # for joins
  refset_tocompare <- refset
  colnames(refset_tocompare)[colnames(refset_tocompare) == "hgvsp"] <- "hgvs_pro"
  refSet_noPHD1_tocompare <- refset_tocompare[!(refset_tocompare$pos >= 296 & refset_tocompare$pos <= 343), ]
  
  #save refsets for LLRp later
  refset_LLRp <- refset_tocompare
  colnames(refset_LLRp)[colnames(refset_LLRp) == "hgvs_pro"] <- "hgvsp"
  refset_LLRp_noPHD1 <- refSet_noPHD1_tocompare
  colnames(refset_LLRp_noPHD1)[colnames(refset_LLRp_noPHD1) == "hgvs_pro"] <- "hgvsp"
  write.csv(refset_LLRp, "refset_for_LLRp.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(refset_LLRp_noPHD1, "refset_for_LLRp_noPHD1.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  
  # 1. map intersection
  intersection_map_refSet <- map %>%
    inner_join(refset_tocompare, by = "hgvs_pro")
  
  # 2. full intersection
  intersection_refSet <- AM %>%
    inner_join(REVEL, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(ESM, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(VAR, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(SIFT, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(map, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(refset_tocompare, by = "hgvs_pro")
  
  # 3. no PHD1
 intersection_refSet_noPHD1 <- intersection_refSet[!(intersection_refSet$pos.x >= 296 & intersection_refSet$pos.x <= 343), ]
  
  # 4. no REVEL
  intersection_refSet_noREVEL <- AM %>%
    inner_join(ESM, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(VAR, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(SIFT, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(map, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(refset_tocompare, by = "hgvs_pro")
  
  # 5. no PHD1 + no REVEL
  intersection_refSet_noPHD1_noREVEL <- AM %>%
    inner_join(ESM, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(VAR, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(SIFT, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(map, by = "hgvs_pro") %>%
    inner_join(refSet_noPHD1_tocompare, by = "hgvs_pro")
  
  # ---- Save outputs ----
  # base filename
  base <- paste0("20250826_", "clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar")
  
  colnames(intersection_refSet)[colnames(intersection_refSet) == "hgvs_pro"] <- "hgvsp"
  colnames(intersection_refSet_noPHD1)[colnames(intersection_refSet_noPHD1) == "hgvs_pro"] <- "hgvsp"
  colnames(intersection_refSet_noREVEL)[colnames(intersection_refSet_noREVEL) == "hgvs_pro"] <- "hgvsp"
  colnames(intersection_refSet_noPHD1_noREVEL)[colnames(intersection_refSet_noPHD1_noREVEL) == "hgvs_pro"] <- "hgvsp"
    
  write.csv(intersection_refSet[, c("hgvsp","referenceSet")],
            paste0(base, "_intersection_full_measuredinBothMaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(intersection_refSet_noPHD1[, c("hgvsp","referenceSet")],
            paste0(base, "_intersection_noPHD1_measuredinBothMaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(intersection_refSet_noREVEL[, c("hgvsp","referenceSet")],
            paste0(base, "_intersection_noREVEL_measuredinBothMaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(intersection_refSet_noPHD1_noREVEL[, c("hgvsp","referenceSet")],
            paste0(base, "_intersection_noPHD1_noREVEL_measuredinBothMaps.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
  
    # Save also the counts (tables) with labels
  sink(paste0(base, "_counts.txt"))
  cat("Counts for ", "clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar", "\n\n")
  cat("1. Variants from reference set measured in map:\n")
  print(table(intersection_map_refSet$referenceSet))
  cat("\n")
  cat("2. Full intersection (AM + REVEL + ESM + VAR + SIFT + map + refset):\n")
  print(table(intersection_refSet$referenceSet))
  cat("\n")
  cat("3. Intersection with PHD1 variants excluded:\n")
  print(table(intersection_refSet_noPHD1$referenceSet))
  cat("\n")
  cat("4. Intersection with REVEL excluded:\n")
  print(table(intersection_refSet_noREVEL$referenceSet))
  cat("\n")
  cat("5. Intersection with both PHD1 and REVEL excluded:\n")
  print(table(intersection_refSet_noPHD1_noREVEL$referenceSet))
  cat("\n")
  sink()

# # ==============================
# # PRC runs for clinvar_onlyKnown_oneStar
# # ==============================
# 
# draw PRC with everything inclusive
#tsm drawPRC Rep_Intersection_merged_map.csv 20250826_clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar_intersection_full_measuredinBothMaps.csv \ --predictors #20240122_ESM1B_maveDB.csv,20240122_AM_maveDB.csv,20240122_varity_maveDB.csv,20240122_sift_maveDB.csv,20260406_REVEL.csv \ --predictorNames #ESM1b,AlphaMissense,VARITY,SIFT,REVEL
# 
# # draw PRC no PHD1 variants
# tsm drawPRC Rep_Intersection_merged_map.csv 20250826_clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar_intersection_noPHD1_measuredinBothMaps.csv \
#   --predictors 20240122_ESM1B_maveDB.csv,20240122_AM_maveDB.csv,20240122_varity_maveDB.csv,20240122_sift_maveDB.csv,20260406_REVEL.csv \
#   --predictorNames ESM1b,AlphaMissense,VARITY,SIFT,REVEL

# # draw PRC with circular evidence strength removed for reviewer comment
# tsm drawPRC Rep_Intersection_merged_map.csv 20260414_clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar_intersection_full_measuredinBothMaps_noCircularity.csv \
#   --predictors 20240122_ESM1B_maveDB.csv,20240122_AM_maveDB.csv,20240122_varity_maveDB.csv,20240122_sift_maveDB.csv,20260406_REVEL.csv \
#   --predictorNames ESM1b,AlphaMissense,VARITY,SIFT,REVEL  
```


```{r LLRp_calculations_FIGURE_filterOn_OnlyMeasuredInBothMaps, echo = FALSE}
#This chunk translates scores to evidence strengths of pathogenicity, but using calcLLR on terminal, instructions below to make Figure S15

#https://github.com/rothlab/docs/wiki/Translating-scores-to-pathogenicity-LLRs
#bandwidths were selected based on attempting with all options that generate a smoother line

#remove outlier scores that are very negative in AIRE map for LLRp plot. The figure does not change as the ones outside this range would be undetermined, just the axis are more appropriate without these variants. They get reappended to the final table as scores.  
map <- read.csv("Rep_Intersection_merged_map.csv")
map_nolow <- map[map$score>=-1.5&map$score<=2.5,]
write.csv(map_nolow, "20250906_WholeMap_noLow_Intersect_bothReps.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#check intersection of map with refset to see how many are on below plots
  #side note: find number of refset measured in map
  map_aside <- map
    colnames(map_aside)[colnames(map_aside) == "hgvs_pro"] <- "hgvsp"
  intersection_map_refSet_unfiltered <- map_aside %>%
    inner_join(refset_LLRp, by = "hgvsp")
   intersection_map_refSet_filteredPHD1 <- map_aside %>%
    inner_join(refset_LLRp_noPHD1, by = "hgvsp")
   table(intersection_map_refSet_unfiltered$referenceSet)
      table(intersection_map_refSet_filteredPHD1$referenceSet)
   
#run in terminal:
# tsm calcLLR -i 10 20250906_WholeMap_noLow_Intersect_bothReps.csv refset_for_LLRp.csv --bandwidth nrd --outlierSuppression 0.1
# tsm calcLLR -i 10 20250906_WholeMap_noLow_Intersect_bothReps.csv refset_for_LLRp_noPHD1.csv --bandwidth nrd --outlierSuppression 0.1
# 
# get thresholds - exclude PHD1 variants
# tsm calcLLR --bandwidth nrd 20250906_WholeMap_noLow_Intersect_bothReps.csv refset_for_LLRp_noPHD1.csv --printTransitions --outlierSuppression 0.1
# 
# Resulting thresholds (exclude PHD1 variants):
#-0.733 : none -> patho.support
#-0.701 : patho.support -> patho.moderate
#-0.599 : patho.moderate -> patho.strong
#-0.110 : patho.strong -> patho.moderate
#0.150 : patho.moderate -> patho.support
#0.371 : patho.support -> none
#0.667 : none -> benign.support
#0.915 : benign.support -> benign.strong
#1.565 : benign.strong -> benign.support
#1.699 : benign.support -> none

####Use the non-PHD1 variant transitions to assign evidence strengths to scores
#load in variants with LLR map scores
score_file <- read.csv("refset_for_LLRp_llr.csv", header = TRUE)

score_file_w_lowandhigh <- bind_rows(
  score_file,
  anti_join(map, score_file, by = "hgvs_pro")
)

#convert all scores to evidence weights
#assigning categories based on whether value is between the thresholds
score_file_ew <- score_file_w_lowandhigh %>%
  mutate(evidence_weight = case_when(
    score <= -0.733 ~ "undetermined",
    score > -0.733 & score <= -0.701 ~ "patho.support",
    score > -0.701 & score <= -0.599 ~ "patho.moderate",
    score > -0.599 & score <= -0.110 ~ "patho.strong",
    score > -0.110 & score <= 0.150 ~ "patho.moderate",
    score > 0.150 & score <= 0.371 ~ "patho.support",    
    score > 0.371 & score <= 0.667 ~ "undetermined",
    score > 0.667 & score <= 0.915 ~ "benign.support",
    score > 0.915 & score <= 1.565 ~ "benign.strong",
    score > 1.565 & score <= 1.699 ~ "benign.support",
    score > 1.699 ~"undetermined"
  ))

#if the variants were out of range to get an LLR calculated, here they are made ND
score_file_ew$llr[is.na(score_file_ew$llr)] <- NA
score_file_ew$llrCI[is.na(score_file_ew$llrCI)] <- NA

#How many missense variants were captured by the map
score_file_missense <- score_file_ew[!(grepl("=", score_file_ew$hgvs_pro)|grepl("Ter", score_file_ew$hgvs_pro)),]
nrow(score_file_missense)

#Load in ClinVar reference set and pull out which is which (fetched Sept 2, 2025)
refset <- read.table("202509_clinvar_result_all_missense.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t")

#Change variant notation to hgvs_pro
#Change colname
names(refset)[names(refset) == 'Protein.change'] <- 'hgvs_pro'
names(refset)[names(refset) == 'Germline.classification'] <- 'sig'
#Change variant notation to hgvs_pro
refset$hgvs_pro <- mgsub(refset$hgvs_pro, pattern = c("A", "R", "N", "D", "C", "Q", "E", 
                                                                      "G", "H", "I", "L", "K", "M", "F", "P",
                                                                      "S", "T", "W", "Y", "V"), 
                                 replacement = c("Ala", "Arg", "Asn", "Asp", "Cys", "Gln",
                                                 "Glu", "Gly", "His", "Ile", "Leu", "Lys",
                                                 "Met", "Phe", "Pro", "Ser",
                                                 "Thr", "Trp", "Tyr", "Val"))
#add p. to notation
refset$hgvs_pro <- paste0("p.", refset$hgvs_pro)

#assigning categories based on what the classification is on ClinVar
refset <- refset %>%
  mutate(type = case_when(
    grepl("enign", refset$sig) ~ "B/LB",
    grepl("Conflicting", refset$sig) ~ "Conflicting",
    grepl("Uncertain", refset$sig) ~ "VUS",
    grepl("not provided", refset$sig) ~ "NA",
    TRUE ~ "P/LP" #Had to do this to include path, because the conflicts say "pathogenicity" in them
  ))

#Keep only required columns from clinvar then merge with known scores to see how the classifications are falling into categories in the map
refset <- refset[, c("hgvs_pro", "type")]
#exclude anything with NA sig
refset_simple <- subset(refset, type != "NA")

#only include refset variants once
refset_simple_test <- refset_simple[duplicated(refset_simple$hgvs_pro),]
#there are duplicates, remove them. All duplicates have 2X VUS entries, except for Ala399Asp, which has one conflict and one VUS, which has been called conflict.keeping just one entry keeps it as a conflict
refset_simple <- refset_simple[!duplicated(refset_simple$hgvs_pro),]

#add scores and LLR evidence weights to the clinvar set
names(refset_simple)[names(refset_simple) == 'hgvsp'] <- 'hgvs_pro'
forsave_labelled_clinvarVariants <- merge(refset_simple, score_file_missense, by="hgvs_pro", all.x=TRUE)
write.csv(forsave_labelled_clinvarVariants, "20250902_ClinVar_labelledLLRps_scores.csv")

#Merge the refset with the LLR values, for all variants
score_file_ew_types <- merge(score_file_ew, refset_simple, by = "hgvs_pro", all.x = TRUE)

##Remove all variants of the PHD1 domain that were not given at least supporting path evidence strength
#prepare regular expression
regexp <- "[[:digit:]]+"
#process position
score_file_ew_types$pos <- str_extract(score_file_ew_types$hgvs_pro, regexp)
score_file_ew_types$pos <- as.numeric(score_file_ew_types$pos)
#assign PHD1 variant class
score_file_ew_types$class <- ifelse(score_file_ew_types$pos >= 296 & score_file_ew_types$pos <= 343, "PHD1", "")
#if the variants are synonymous, nonsense or are in the PHD1 domain and not patho, change them to undetermined
score_file_ew_types <- score_file_ew_types %>%
  mutate(
    evidence_weight = case_when(
      grepl("=", hgvs_pro) ~ NA,
      grepl("Ter", hgvs_pro) ~ NA,
      class == "PHD1" & !grepl("^patho", evidence_weight) ~ "undetermined",
      TRUE ~ evidence_weight
    ),
    llr = case_when(
      grepl("=", hgvs_pro) ~ NA,
      grepl("Ter", hgvs_pro) ~ NA,
      TRUE ~ llr 
    ),
    llrCI = case_when(
      grepl("=", hgvs_pro) ~ NA,
      grepl("Ter", hgvs_pro) ~ NA,
      TRUE ~ llrCI
    )
  )

#format the LLRCI to be readable for maveDB
#remove square brackets
score_file_ew_types$llrCI <- gsub("\\[|\\]", "", score_file_ew_types$llrCI)
#separate into two columns
score_file_ew_types <- score_file_ew_types %>%
  separate(llrCI, into = c("llrCI_lower", "llrCI_upper"), sep = ";", convert = TRUE)
#If lower is ND, make upper also ND.
score_file_ew_types$llrCI_upper[is.na(score_file_ew_types$llrCI_lower)] <- NA

#generate file to upload to maveDB. this is all scores, inlcuding nonsense/synonymous, and only the variants measured in both biological replicates
#save final scores with evidence weights, setting PHD1 variants to undetermined. these do not have SNV label
write.csv(score_file_ew_types, "20250913_finalScoreswEvidenceWeights_noSNVlabel.csv", row.names=FALSE)
```

```{r SNV-accessible_basedOnSeq, echo=FALSE}
#this script makes a column in the dataset that says whether your sequence is snv-accessible based on the actual AIRE coding sequence (non-codon optimized)
#this script also makes the file required for maveDB score upload, with all necessary columns

### --- 1. Prepare coding sequence and codons --- ###
cDS <- ""

codons <- substring(cDS, seq(1,nchar(cDS)-2,3), seq(3,nchar(cDS),3))
pos <- seq_along(codons)
SNV_table <- data.frame(pos, codons, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Remove stop codon at end
stop_codons <- c("TAA","TAG","TGA")
if(tail(SNV_table$codons,1) %in% stop_codons) SNV_table <- SNV_table[-nrow(SNV_table), ]

# Translation tables
codon_table <- c(
  "TTT"="F","TTC"="F","TTA"="L","TTG"="L","TCT"="S","TCC"="S","TCA"="S","TCG"="S",
  "TAT"="Y","TAC"="Y","TAA"="*","TAG"="*","TGT"="C","TGC"="C","TGA"="*","TGG"="W",
  "CTT"="L","CTC"="L","CTA"="L","CTG"="L","CCT"="P","CCC"="P","CCA"="P","CCG"="P",
  "CAT"="H","CAC"="H","CAA"="Q","CAG"="Q","CGT"="R","CGC"="R","CGA"="R","CGG"="R",
  "ATT"="I","ATC"="I","ATA"="I","ATG"="M","ACT"="T","ACC"="T","ACA"="T","ACG"="T",
  "AAT"="N","AAC"="N","AAA"="K","AAG"="K","AGT"="S","AGC"="S","AGA"="R","AGG"="R",
  "GTT"="V","GTC"="V","GTA"="V","GTG"="V","GCT"="A","GCC"="A","GCA"="A","GCG"="A",
  "GAT"="D","GAC"="D","GAA"="E","GAG"="E","GGT"="G","GGC"="G","GGA"="G","GGG"="G"
)
aa_1to3 <- c(A="Ala", R="Arg", N="Asn", D="Asp", C="Cys", Q="Gln", E="Glu", G="Gly",
             H="His", I="Ile", L="Leu", K="Lys", M="Met", F="Phe", P="Pro", S="Ser",
             T="Thr", W="Trp", Y="Tyr", V="Val")
SNV_table$aa <- codon_table[SNV_table$codons]

### --- 2. Generate all SNVs --- ###
get_possible_snvs <- function(codon) {
  bases <- c("A","C","G","T")
  snvs <- character()
  for(i in 1:3) for(b in bases[bases != substr(codon,i,i)]) {
    new_codon <- codon; substr(new_codon,i,i) <- b; snvs <- c(snvs,new_codon)
  }
  unique(snvs)
}

SNV_table$possible_SNVs <- lapply(SNV_table$codons,get_possible_snvs)
SNV_table$possible_AAs <- lapply(SNV_table$possible_SNVs,function(cds) codon_table[cds])
SNV_table$all_SNV_accessible_AA <- lapply(SNV_table$possible_AAs,function(x) unique(x))

SNV_table$possible_SNVs <- sapply(SNV_table$possible_SNVs,paste,collapse=", ")
SNV_table$possible_AAs <- sapply(SNV_table$possible_AAs,paste,collapse=", ")
SNV_table$all_SNV_accessible_AA <- sapply(SNV_table$all_SNV_accessible_AA,paste,collapse=", ")

### --- 3. Build HGVS for missense, synonymous, nonsense --- ###
# Missense
SNV_table$missense <- mapply(function(all_aa, orig) {
  aas <- unlist(strsplit(all_aa,",\\s*")); aas <- aas[aas!=orig & aas!="*"]; paste(aas, collapse=", ")
}, SNV_table$all_SNV_accessible_AA, SNV_table$aa)

# Correct missense HGVS with full three-letter codes
SNV_table$missense_hgvs <- mapply(function(orig,pos,miss) {
  if(miss=="") return(NA)
  aas <- unlist(strsplit(miss,",\\s*"))
  paste0("p.", aa_1to3[orig], pos, aa_1to3[aas], collapse=", ")
}, SNV_table$aa, SNV_table$pos, SNV_table$missense)

# Synonymous
SNV_table$synonymous_hgvs <- mapply(function(orig,pos,all_aa) {
  aas <- unlist(strsplit(all_aa,",\\s*"))
  if(orig %in% aas) paste0("p.",aa_1to3[orig],pos,"=") else NA
}, SNV_table$aa, SNV_table$pos, SNV_table$all_SNV_accessible_AA)

# Nonsense
SNV_table$nonsense_hgvs <- sapply(seq_len(nrow(SNV_table)), function(i) {
  aas <- unlist(strsplit(SNV_table$all_SNV_accessible_AA[i], ",\\s*"))
  aas <- trimws(aas)
  if("*" %in% aas) {
    paste0("p.", aa_1to3[SNV_table$aa[i]], SNV_table$pos[i], "Ter")
  } else {
    NA
  }
})

### --- 4. Combine all into one table --- ###
make_variant_df <- function(hgvs_col,type_label){
  variant_lists <- strsplit(hgvs_col,",\\s*")
  variant_lists <- lapply(variant_lists,function(x)x[x!="" & !is.na(x)])
  do.call(rbind,lapply(seq_along(variant_lists), function(i){
    if(length(variant_lists[[i]])==0) return(NULL)
    data.frame(pos=SNV_table$pos[i], orig_aa=SNV_table$aa[i],
               variant=variant_lists[[i]], type=type_label, stringsAsFactors=FALSE)
  }))
}

missense_df <- make_variant_df(SNV_table$missense_hgvs,"missense")
syn_df <- make_variant_df(SNV_table$synonymous_hgvs,"synonymous")
nonsense_df <- make_variant_df((SNV_table$nonsense_hgvs), "nonsense")


all_SNV_df <- rbind(missense_df,syn_df,nonsense_df)
write.csv(all_SNV_df,"all_SNV_variants_unified.csv",row.names=FALSE)

### --- 5. Mark SNV-accessible variants in scores --- ###
#first attach the variants seen in just one biological replicate
oneReponly <- read.csv("uncommonReps.csv")
score_file_ew_types <- bind_rows(
  oneReponly  %>% mutate(numberofBioReps = 1),
  score_file_ew_types %>% mutate(numberofBioReps = 2)
)
score_file_ew_types$SNVaccessible <- ifelse(
  score_file_ew_types$hgvs_pro %in% all_SNV_df$variant, "yes","no"
)
score_file_ew_types <- score_file_ew_types[order(score_file_ew_types$pos), ]
#mark as ND if they have only one bio rep
score_file_ew_types <- score_file_ew_types %>%
  mutate(
    llr = ifelse(numberofBioReps == 1, NA, llr),
    llrCI_lower = ifelse(numberofBioReps == 1, NA, llrCI_lower),
    llrCI_upper = ifelse(numberofBioReps == 1, NA, llrCI_upper),
    evidence_weight = ifelse(numberofBioReps == 1, NA, evidence_weight)
  )

#############THE BELOW IS THE FINAL FILE FOR THE MAVEDB UPLOAD!########################
#format this so that the "class" changes to PHD1variant, yes and no. clean up
score_file_ew_types_maveDB <- score_file_ew_types %>%
  mutate(PHD1variant = ifelse(!is.na(class) & class == "PHD1", "yes", "no")) %>%
  select(-class)
score_file_ew_types_maveDB <- score_file_ew_types_maveDB %>%
 mutate(
    llrCI_lower = ifelse(llrCI_lower == "0.000", "0", llrCI_lower),
    llrCI_upper = ifelse(llrCI_upper == "0.000", "0", llrCI_upper)
  )
#submission does not like mixed characters and numeric types
score_file_ew_types_maveDB$llr <- as.character(score_file_ew_types_maveDB$llr)
score_file_ew_types_maveDB$llrCI_lower <- as.character(score_file_ew_types_maveDB$llrCI_lower)
score_file_ew_types_maveDB$llrCI_upper <- as.character(score_file_ew_types_maveDB$llrCI_upper)

#incorporate classification information into dataset 
classifications <- read.csv("ClinVar_reclassifications_EiBr_041125.csv", header = TRUE)
classifications$hgvs_pro <- sub(",.*", "", classifications$hgvs_pro.score.se.df.llr.llrCI_lower.llrCI_upper.evidence_weight.ClinVarType.pos.class.SNVaccessible)
#remove the long column, only include necessary info
classifications_toMerge <- classifications[,-c(1:9)]
#replace all dashes with NA
classifications_toMerge[classifications_toMerge == "-"] <- NA
#append classifications to MaveDB upload
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final <- merge(score_file_ew_types_maveDB, classifications_toMerge, by = "hgvs_pro", all = TRUE)
#replace generic "Conflicting" entries with more detailed ClinVar class values
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$type <- ifelse(
  score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$type == "Conflicting" & 
    !is.na(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$ClinVar.Class),
  score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$ClinVar.Class,
  score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$type
)
#add more info to the ones that are missing conflicting details
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$type[score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$hgvs_pro == "p.Gly306Arg"] <- "Conflicting: VUS, LP"
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$type[score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$hgvs_pro == "p.Val301Met"] <- "Conflicting: VUS, LB"

#Remove the extra VUS that have been added to ClinVar since the reference set was downloaded. 
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final <- score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final[!grepl("Thr391Asn|Leu428Val", score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$hgvs_pro),]
colnames(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final)[colnames(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final) == "type"] <- "ClinVarClass"
colnames(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final)[colnames(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final) == "Reclass."] <- "Reclass"
colnames(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final)[colnames(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final) == "Final.class"] <- "FinalClass"
colnames(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final)[colnames(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final) == "Reclass.due.to.functional.map."] <- "ReclassDueToFunctionalMap"

#how many variants in the APS-1 patient cohort are not in the ClinVar set? Add this flag. Also add the evidence manually for these. 
clinvar_forcohortcompare <- read.csv("20250825_AIRE_missense_clinvar_result.csv")
#change to hgvsp format
clinvar_forcohortcompare$Protein.change <- mgsub(clinvar_forcohortcompare$Protein.change, pattern = c("A", "R", "N", "D", "C", "Q", "E", 
                                      "G", "H", "I", "L", "K", "M", "F", "P",
                                      "S", "T", "W", "Y", "V"), 
                      replacement = c("Ala", "Arg", "Asn", "Asp", "Cys", "Gln",
                                                "Glu", "Gly", "His", "Ile", "Leu", "Lys",
                                                "Met", "Phe", "Pro", "Ser",
                                                "Thr", "Trp", "Tyr", "Val"))
colnames(clinvar_forcohortcompare)[colnames(clinvar_forcohortcompare) == "Protein.change"] ="hgvs_pro"
clinvar_forcohortcompare$hgvs_pro <- paste("p.", clinvar_forcohortcompare$hgvs_pro, sep = "")
#patients
patients_forclinvarcompare <- read.csv("genphen_readable.csv")
 # Remove all brackets from dataset
  patients_forclinvarcompare <- data.frame(apply(patients_forclinvarcompare, 2, function(x) gsub("[(&)]", "", x)))
  allele_vector <- c(patients_forclinvarcompare$allele.1.protein, patients_forclinvarcompare$allele.2.protein)
#find variants not in clinvar from patient set
unique_patient_variants <- setdiff(unique(allele_vector), clinvar_forcohortcompare$hgvs_pro)
unique_patient_variants

####section to add in the manually novel variants into the dataframe. 
patientVarannotated <- read.csv("Manual_addPatientVar.csv")
#make the llr a numeric vale
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$llr <- as.numeric(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$llr)
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$llrCI_lower <- as.numeric(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$llrCI_lower)
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$llrCI_upper <- as.numeric(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$llrCI_upper)

# Add the missing "ClinVar.Class" column to patientVarannotated, filling with NA
patientVarannotated <- patientVarannotated %>%
  mutate(ClinVar.Class = NA)

# Combine the data frames, giving precedence to patientVarannotated for matching hgvs_pro
# Step 1: Deduplicate patient variants first by hgvs_pro
patientVarannotated_unique <- patientVarannotated %>%
  group_by(hgvs_pro) %>%
  slice(1) %>%  # keep the first row if there are multiple
  ungroup()

# Step 2: Exclude any patient variants from the original score file
non_patient_variants <- score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final %>%
  filter(!hgvs_pro %in% patientVarannotated_unique$hgvs_pro)

# Step 3: Combine
combined_df <- bind_rows(non_patient_variants, patientVarannotated_unique)

# Step 4: Ensure column order matches
combined_df <- combined_df %>% select(all_of(colnames(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final)))

# Step 5: Optional check for duplicates
any(duplicated(combined_df$hgvs_pro))  # should return FALSE

# Optional: Ensure column order matches the original score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final <- combined_df %>%
  select(all_of(colnames(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final)))

# Add "CAUTION" flags for variants whose CI overlaps the midpoint of undetermined (uncertain variants)
df <- combined_df
# only include rows where evidence weight is non-undetermined
valid <- !df$evidence_weight %in% c(NA, "undetermined")
# Compute CI bounds
ci_low  <- df$score - df$se
ci_high <- df$score + df$se
# Overlaps midpoint? (TRUE if CI contains the midpoint)
overlaps_ci <- function(ref) ci_low < ref & ci_high > ref
# Apply flags only to valid rows, midpoint of the thing
df$evidenceWeightConfidence <- ifelse(valid & overlaps_ci(0.5), "CAUTION", NA)
# Save back
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final <- df
table(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$evidenceWeightConfidence)

#sort by pos
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final <- score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final[order(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$pos), ]
#remove less detailed ClinVar annotation (conflicts were not as detailed before)
score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final <- score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final[, -which(names(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final) == "ClinVar.Class")]
any(duplicated(score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final$hgvs_pro))
#save for maveDB upload (this file is not to be used to be uploaded, save separately to be used in this script, because the quotes contradict with upload, but then mess up the rest)
readr::write_csv(
  score_file_ew_types_maveDB_final,
  "20251110_finalScoreswEvidenceWeights_test.csv",
  quote = "needed"
)

#summarize the ones with the caution flag
caution_only <- score_file_ew_types_maveDB[score_file_ew_types_maveDB$flag1=="CAUTION",]
table(caution_only$evidence_weight)
sum(table(caution_only$evidence_weight))

```

```{r identify_significantly_upreg_var, echo = FALSE}
##this chunk extracts hyper-active variants and identifies significant ones
#identify the synonymous variant vector
synonymous <- which(grepl("=", scores$hgvs_pro))
syn.mean <- mean(scores$score[synonymous], na.rm = TRUE)
#previous variant of less-stringent synonymous variant vector - this lets in more variants. not used for paper
#syn.err <- sd(scores$score[synonymous], na.rm = TRUE)/sqrt(length(synonymous))
syn.err <- sd(scores$score[synonymous], na.rm = TRUE)

#manual significance test for candidate gain of function variants, taking error into account
welch.test <- function(mean1,err1,mean2,err2,n1,n2) {
  t <- (mean1-mean2)/sqrt(err1^2 + err2^2)
  p <- pt(t,n1+n2-1,lower.tail=TRUE)
  return(p)
}

#extract p vals from variants
p.vals <- mapply(welch.test, syn.mean, syn.err, scores$score, scores$se, length(synonymous), 4)
#multiple testing correction
p.adj <- p.adjust(p.vals,length(scores$score),method="fdr")
hyper.idx <- which(p.adj < 0.05)
sig_scores <- scores[which(rownames(scores) %in% hyper.idx), ]
colnames(sig_scores)[colnames(sig_scores) == "AA_change"] <- "hgvs_pro"
write.csv(file = "sig_hyper.csv", sig_scores, row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r evidenceWeightforFigurelaterand_otherQs, echo = FALSE}
#This chunk generates a dataframe required to make the plot of evidence weight summaries, and answers in-text questions for the paper
#how many SNV variants have evidence that is not undetermined? 
score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly <- score_file_ew_types[!score_file_ew_types$evidence_weight == "undetermined",]
score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly <- score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly[score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly$SNVaccessible == "yes",]
#how many variants are SNV accessible and have evidence other than undetermined, and no PHD1 domain?
```
