```{r evidenceWeightforFigurelaterand_otherQs, echo = FALSE}
score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_noPHD1 <- score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly[!score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly$class == "PHD1",]
#how many of the SNVs not in PHD1 are VUS with evidence? 
score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_noPHD1_VUSonly <- na.omit(score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_noPHD1[score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_noPHD1$type == "VUS",])
#proportion of total VUS captured no PHD1 domain
nrow(score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_noPHD1_VUSonly)
table(score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_noPHD1_VUSonly$evidence_weight)
print(nrow(score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_noPHD1_VUSonly)/314)
#how many variants are SNV accessible and they are patho evidence? not just VUS
score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_patho <- score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly[
  grepl("patho", score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly$evidence_weight), 
]
#how many PHD1 domain variants have evidence towards patho?
score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_patho_PHD1only <- score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_patho[score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_patho$class == "PHD1",]
nrow(score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_patho_PHD1only)
#how many VUS have evidence strength with and without the PHD1 domain?
score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_VUSonly <- na.omit(score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly[score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly$type == "VUS",])
#include only evidence towards pathogenicity
score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_towardPatho <- score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_VUSonly[
  grepl("patho", score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_VUSonly$evidence_weight), 
]
#VUS number with evidence strength with PHD1 domain
print(nrow(score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_towardPatho))
#table for how many VUS with evidence with no PHD1 domain
table(score_file_ew_types_no_undetermined_SNVonly_towardPatho$class)

##Eliminate ALL PHD1 variants not considering the evidence to draw alluvial plot #1
score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1 <- score_file_ew_types[!score_file_ew_types$class == "PHD1",]
```

```{r plot_stackedbargraph, echo = FALSE}
#This chunk makes the stacked barplot for Figure 3B
##plot #2 is include anything in the PHD1 domain given at least supporting path evidence
#identify vector of hgvs_pros that are PHD1 and have patho evidence strength
PHD1_variants_KEEP <- score_file_ew_types[score_file_ew_types$class == "PHD1" & grepl("path", score_file_ew_types$evidence_weight),]
#what proportion of PHD1 variants were given patho supporting evidence or worse?
PHD1_only <- score_file_ew_types[score_file_ew_types$class == "PHD1",]
nrow(PHD1_variants_KEEP)/nrow(PHD1_only)
#remove PHD1 variants that are not in the KEEP list
PHD1_variants_REMOVE <- score_file_ew_types[score_file_ew_types$class == "PHD1" & !(grepl("path", score_file_ew_types$evidence_weight)),]
PHD1_variants_REMOVE_hgvspro <- PHD1_variants_REMOVE$hgvs_pro
#generate final file with PHD1 variants only included if they are given damaging evidence strengths
final_variants_with_evidenceWeights <- score_file_ew_types[!score_file_ew_types$hgvs_pro %in% PHD1_variants_REMOVE_hgvspro,]
#how many variants given evidence weights including undetermined
nrow(final_variants_with_evidenceWeights)
#proportion of these variants that are SNV-accessible
table(final_variants_with_evidenceWeights$SNVaccessible)[2]
table(final_variants_with_evidenceWeights$SNVaccessible)[2]/(table(final_variants_with_evidenceWeights$SNVaccessible)[2]+table(final_variants_with_evidenceWeights$SNVaccessible)[1])

###How many VUS were given evidence strengths? 
#remove all PHD1 in general
score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1_VUS <- score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1[grepl("VUS",score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1$type),]
table_VUSbreakdown_noPHD1 <- table(score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1_VUS$evidence_weight)
write.csv(table_VUSbreakdown_noPHD1, "table_VUS_Breakdown_evidence_elimallPHD1.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#inclusive of PHD1 path evidence
final_variants_with_evidenceWeights_VUS <- final_variants_with_evidenceWeights[grepl("VUS",final_variants_with_evidenceWeights$type),]
table_VUSbreakdown <- table(final_variants_with_evidenceWeights_VUS$evidence_weight)
write.csv(table_VUSbreakdown, "table_VUS_Breakdown_evidence_includePHD1pathevidence.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Color-blind friendly color palette from RColorBrewer
color_palette <- brewer.pal(7, "Paired")

###Plot stacked bargraph to illustrate variant evidence strength types (including all PHD1 with path evidence)
#####process files for all variants, but remove anyone with only one bio rep 
score_file_ew_types_all_forstackedbarplot <- score_file_ew_types[score_file_ew_types$numberofBioReps==2, ]
score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1_all_forstackedbarplot <- 
final_variants_with_evidenceWeights[final_variants_with_evidenceWeights$numberofBioReps==2, ]
score_file_ew_types_all_forstackedbarplot <- score_file_ew_types_all_forstackedbarplot[!is.na(score_file_ew_types_all_forstackedbarplot$evidence_weight),]
score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1_all_forstackedbarplot <- score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1_all_forstackedbarplot[!is.na(score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1_all_forstackedbarplot$evidence_weight),]
#convert evidence_weight to a factor to keep order
score_file_ew_types_all_forstackedbarplot$evidence_weight <- factor(
  score_file_ew_types_all_forstackedbarplot$evidence_weight,
  levels = c("benign.strong", "benign.support", "undetermined", "patho.support", "patho.moderate", "patho.strong", "patho.vstrong"))
score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1_all_forstackedbarplot$evidence_weight <- factor(
  score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1_all_forstackedbarplot$evidence_weight,
  levels = c("benign.strong", "benign.support", "undetermined", "patho.support", "patho.moderate", "patho.strong", "patho.vstrong"))


 #assign colours to evidence weights
color_palette <- c(
  "benign.support" = "#b5dda6",  
  "benign.strong" = "#75a85b",  
  "patho.support" = "#c8acfc",     
  "patho.moderate" = "#8d75d8",
  "patho.strong" = "#7a43aa",
  "undetermined" = "#c9c9c9"       
)

  #draw this plot as a stacked barplot, match colours to previous plot 
p1 <- ggplot(score_file_ew_types_all_forstackedbarplot, aes(x = type, fill = evidence_weight)) + 
    geom_bar(position = "fill") +  
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) + 
    theme_minimal(base_size = 17) +
    labs(y = "Proportion", x = "current ClinVar annotation") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format())  # format y axis as %
#Save stacked barplot
ggsave("stackedBarplot_includePHD1pathevidence.pdf", p1, width = 6, height = 5)
print(p1)

  #draw this plot as a stacked barplot, match colours to previous plot 
p2 <- ggplot(score_file_ew_types_no_PHD1_all_forstackedbarplot, aes(x = type, fill = evidence_weight)) + 
    geom_bar(position = "fill") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) + 
    theme_minimal(base_size = 17) +
    labs(y = "Proportion", x = "current ClinVar annotation") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format())  # format y axis as %
#Save stacked barplot
ggsave("stacked_barplotNoPHD1.pdf", p2, width = 6, height = 5)
print(p2)

#these plots are the same because no damaging variants with PHD1 clinvar included
#how many non-ClinVar variants got evidence strengths, including those of the PHD1 domain that have damaging scores?
#this only gives evidence strengths that are patho to PHD1 domain, otherwise they are not in this list
final_variants_with_evidenceWeights_nonClinvar <-  final_variants_with_evidenceWeights[is.na(final_variants_with_evidenceWeights$type), ]
```

```{r AIRE_interactors, fig.show='hold', echo=FALSE}
#This chunk extracts interacting positions for AIRE dimers, trimers and tetramers, makes a summary table of interacting positions, and makes boxplot of scores for each of these categories for Figure S11

# Load map
scores_for_interacts <- read.csv("20231018_WholeMap.csv", header=TRUE)
# Subset only missense variants
scores_missense <- scores_for_interacts %>%
  filter(!grepl("=", hgvs_pro) & !grepl("Ter", hgvs_pro))
# Extract position from hgvs_pro
scores_missense$pos <- as.numeric(str_extract(scores_missense$hgvs_pro, "[0-9]+"))

#draw boxplot of scores whether they are interacting vs non-interacting, print median, save interactors to file
analyze_data_interaction_status <- function(data, title, width, height) {
  
  # Create the boxplot to compare interacting vs non-interacting
  p_boxplot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = interaction_status, y = score)) + 
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(title = title, x = "interaction status", y = "functional score") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
  
  # Save the combined plot
  print(p_boxplot)
  ggsave(filename = paste0(title, "_boxplot.pdf"), plot = p_boxplot, width = width, height = height * 1.5)
  
  # Get unique interaction categories ("interacting" and "non-interacting")
  categories <- unique(data$interaction_status)
  
  # Initialize an empty dataframe to store the results
  results <- data.frame(Category1 = character(), Category2 = character(), 
                        p.value = numeric(), estimate = numeric(), 
                        Median1 = numeric(), Median2 = numeric())
  
  # Initialize a list to store significant comparisons
  comparisons <- list()
  
  # Loop through each combination of two categories
  for(i in 1:(length(categories)-1)){
    for(j in (i+1):length(categories)){
      category1 <- categories[i]
      category2 <- categories[j]
      
      # Subset the data for the two categories
      category1_data <- data[data$interaction_status == category1,]
      category2_data <- data[data$interaction_status == category2,]
      
      # Perform the Wilcoxon test
      test_result <- wilcox.test(category1_data$score, category2_data$score, 
                                 mu=0, alt="two.sided", conf.int=T, conf.level = 0.95, 
                                 paired=F, correct=T)
      
      # Extract the p-value and estimate
      p_value <- test_result$p.value
      estimate <- test_result$estimate
      
      # Calculate medians
      median1 <- median(category1_data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
      median2 <- median(category2_data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      # Add the results to the dataframe
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Category1 = category1, Category2 = category2, 
                                           p.value = p_value, estimate = estimate, 
                                           Median1 = median1, Median2 = median2))
      
      # Add the comparison for significance testing
      comparisons <- append(comparisons, list(c(category1, category2)))
    }
  }
  
  # Get the number of tests performed
  n_tests <- nrow(results)
  
  # Apply the Bonferroni correction to the p-values
  results$p.value <- p.adjust(results$p.value, method = "bonferroni")
  
  # Output only significant results
  sig_results <- results[results$p.value < 0.05,]
  
  # Add significance bars if there are significant results
  if (nrow(sig_results) > 0) {
    sig_comparisons <- comparisons[results$p.value < 0.05]
    p_boxplot <- p_boxplot + stat_compare_means(comparisons = sig_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", 
                                                label = "p.signif", p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
    print(p_boxplot)
    ggsave(p_boxplot, device = "pdf", filename = paste0(title, "_boxplot_with_significance.pdf"), width = width, height = height * 1.5)
  }
  
  # Write significant results to a CSV file
  write.csv(file = paste0(title, ".csv"), sig_results, row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Print the medians for both interacting and non-interacting categories
  cat("Medians of categories:\n")
  for(category in categories) {
    median_value <- median(data[data$interaction_status == category,]$score, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("Category:", category, "Median:", median_value, "\n")
  }
}

###AIRE DIMER
# Load interacting positions
interacting <- read.table("dimer_test_24e06_unrelaxed_rank_003_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
# Convert interacting positions to numeric for comparison
interacting_pos <- unique(c(as.numeric(interacting$V2), as.numeric(interacting$V5)))
#sort interacting pos from low to high and save as interactor file
total_interactings <- c(as.numeric(interacting$V2), as.numeric(interacting$V5))
#print list of interacting positions
table(total_interactings)
table(interacting_pos)
#sort in numeric order
ordered_interactingPos_Dimer <- unique(sort(total_interactings))
#save vector
write.csv(ordered_interactingPos_Dimer, "Dimer_interacting_positions.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Label positions as "interacting" or "non-interacting"
scores_missense$interaction_status <- ifelse(scores_missense$pos %in% interacting_pos, 
                                             "interacting_dimer", "non-interacting")
analyze_data_interaction_status(scores_missense, "AIRE_DIMER", 3, 4)

#TRIMER
# Load interacting positions
# Load map
scores_for_interacts <- read.csv("20231018_WholeMap.csv", header=TRUE)
# Subset only missense variants
scores_missense <- scores_for_interacts %>%
  filter(!grepl("=", hgvs_pro) & !grepl("Ter", hgvs_pro))

interacting <- read.table("AIRE_TRIMER_fe51d_unrelaxed_rank_003_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
interacting_BC <- read.table("BC_AIRE_TRIMER_fe51d_unrelaxed_rank_003_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
interacting_trimer <- rbind(interacting, interacting_BC)

interacting_pos_trimer <- unique(c(as.numeric(interacting_trimer$V2), as.numeric(interacting_trimer$V5)))
total_interactings <- c(as.numeric(interacting_trimer$V2), as.numeric(interacting_trimer$V5))
table(total_interactings)
table(interacting_pos_trimer)

# Subset only missense variants
# Extract position from hgvs_pro
scores_missense$pos <- as.numeric(str_extract(scores_missense$hgvs_pro, "[0-9]+"))
# Label positions as "interacting" or "non-interacting"
scores_missense$interaction_status <- ifelse(scores_missense$pos %in% interacting_pos_trimer, 
                                             "interacting_trimer", "non-interacting")
analyze_data_interaction_status(scores_missense, "AIRE_TRIMER", 3, 4)

#TETRAMER
# Load interacting positions
interacting_1 <- read.table("1_Tetramer_AIRE_4_unrelaxed_rank_002_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
interacting_2 <- read.table("2_Tetramer_AIRE_4_unrelaxed_rank_002_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
interacting_3 <- read.table("3_Tetramer_AIRE_4_unrelaxed_rank_002_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
#tetramer
interacting_tetramer <- rbind(interacting_1, interacting_2, interacting_3)
interacting_pos_tetramer <- unique(c(as.numeric(interacting_tetramer$V2), as.numeric(interacting_tetramer$V5)))

# Subset only missense variants
scores_missense <- scores_for_interacts %>%
  filter(!grepl("=", hgvs_pro) & !grepl("Ter", hgvs_pro))
# Extract position from hgvs_pro
scores_missense$pos <- as.numeric(str_extract(scores_missense$hgvs_pro, "[0-9]+"))
# Label positions as "interacting" or "non-interacting"
scores_missense$interaction_status <- ifelse(scores_missense$pos %in% interacting_pos_tetramer, 
                                             "interacting", "non-interacting")
analyze_data_interaction_status(scores_missense, "AIRE_TETRAMER", 3, 4)


##prepare file for supplementals

#DIMER
interacting_dimer <- read.table("dimer_test_24e06_unrelaxed_rank_003_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
dimer_positions <- sort(unique(c(as.numeric(interacting_dimer$V2),
                                 as.numeric(interacting_dimer$V5))))
#TRIMER
interacting_trimer_A <- read.table("AIRE_TRIMER_fe51d_unrelaxed_rank_003_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
interacting_trimer_B <- read.table("BC_AIRE_TRIMER_fe51d_unrelaxed_rank_003_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
interacting_trimer <- rbind(interacting_trimer_A, interacting_trimer_B)

trimer_positions <- sort(unique(c(as.numeric(interacting_trimer$V2),
                                  as.numeric(interacting_trimer$V5))))
#TETRAMER
interacting_t1 <- read.table("1_Tetramer_AIRE_4_unrelaxed_rank_002_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
interacting_t2 <- read.table("2_Tetramer_AIRE_4_unrelaxed_rank_002_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
interacting_t3 <- read.table("3_Tetramer_AIRE_4_unrelaxed_rank_002_alphafold2_multimer_v3_model_1_seed_000_1.ic")
interacting_tetramer <- rbind(interacting_t1, interacting_t2, interacting_t3)
tetramer_positions <- sort(unique(c(as.numeric(interacting_tetramer$V2),
                                    as.numeric(interacting_tetramer$V5))))
#make table
interaction_table <- tibble(
  Complex = c("dimer", "trimer", "tetramer"),
  positions = c(
    paste(dimer_positions, collapse = ","),
    paste(trimer_positions, collapse = ","),
    paste(tetramer_positions, collapse = ",")
  )
)

write.table(interaction_table,
            "AIRE_interacting_positions.csv",
            sep = ",",
            quote = TRUE,
            row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r rare_vs_common, echo= FALSE}
#This chunk analyzes singleton allele frequency vs all else for the calculation in the manuscript (no figure)
#read in gnomAD data
gnomAD_data <- read.csv("20250825_gnomAD_v4.1.0_ENSG00000160224.csv")
gnomAD_data <- gnomAD_data[,c(12,14,15,20)]
gnomAD_data <- gnomAD_data[gnomAD_data$VEP.Annotation == "missense_variant",]
colnames(gnomAD_data)[colnames(gnomAD_data) == "Protein.Consequence"] ="hgvs_pro"

#see duplicated entries and review manually what is happening
# extract rows with duplicate variants
gnomAD_duplicates <- gnomAD_data[gnomAD_data$hgvs_pro %in% 
                                   gnomAD_data$hgvs_pro[duplicated(gnomAD_data$hgvs_pro)], ]

# inspect
print(head(gnomAD_duplicates))

#there are duplicates, and we don't care about what gnomad says about the clinvar annotation, because we have the clinvars. so just keep first instance
gnomAD_data <- gnomAD_data[!duplicated(gnomAD_data$hgvs_pro), ]

#define gnomAD thresholds for common and rare
freq_common <- 0.000001
#singletons
freq_rare <- 0.000001

#initialize results table
results_table <- data.frame(
  common_threshold = numeric(),
  rare_threshold = numeric(),
  median_common = numeric(),
  median_rare = numeric(),
  p_value = numeric(),
  significant = logical(),
  n_common = integer(),
  n_rare = integer()
)

#loop over thresholds
for (i in seq_along(freq_common)) {
  common_threshold <- freq_common[i]
  rare_threshold <- freq_rare[i]
  
  #categorize variants
  gnomAD_data_noAnnot <- gnomAD_data %>%
    mutate(rarity = case_when(
      Allele.Frequency > common_threshold ~ "common",
      Allele.Frequency <= rare_threshold ~ "rare",
      TRUE ~ NA
    )) %>%
    filter(!is.na(rarity)) 
  
  #merge with scores
  common_rare_wScores <- merge(gnomAD_data_noAnnot, scores, by = "hgvs_pro")
  
  #skip if not enough data
  if (nrow(common_rare_wScores) < 2) next
  
  #calculate medians
  medians <- common_rare_wScores %>%
    group_by(rarity) %>%
    summarise(median_score = median(score, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = rarity, values_from = median_score)
  
  #count variants in each group
  counts <- common_rare_wScores %>%
    count(rarity) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = rarity, values_from = n)
  
  #perform wilcoxon test
  test_result <- wilcox.test(score ~ rarity, data = common_rare_wScores)
  
  #save to results table
  results_table <- rbind(results_table, data.frame(
    common_threshold = common_threshold,
    rare_threshold = rare_threshold,
    median_common = ifelse("common" %in% names(medians), medians$common, NA),
    median_rare = ifelse("rare" %in% names(medians), medians$rare, NA),
    p_value = test_result$p.value,
    significant = test_result$p.value < 0.05,
    n_common = ifelse("common" %in% names(counts), counts$common, 0),
    n_rare = ifelse("rare" %in% names(counts), counts$rare, 0)
  ))
  #create boxplot
  map_commonRare <- ggplot(common_rare_wScores, aes(x = rarity, y = score)) + 
    geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.3) + 
    theme_minimal(base_size = 15) + 
    labs(x = "", y = "variant functional score")
  #add significance bar if significant
  if (test_result$p.value < 0.05) {
    map_commonRare <- map_commonRare + 
      stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif", hide.ns = TRUE)
  }
  #save plot
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("AIRE_common_rare_boxplot_", common_threshold, "_", rare_threshold, ".pdf"), 
    plot = map_commonRare, 
    height = 5, width = 3, device = "pdf"
  )
}

#view results
print(results_table)

#save to csv
write.csv(results_table, "AIRE_common_rare_summary_all_cutoffs.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r lollipop_AIRE, echo = FALSE}
#This script shows discordant and concordant ClinVar scores along the AIRE protein for Figure S16A
clinvar1 <- clinvar_hgvsp_onlyKnown_oneStar
map <- read.csv("Rep_Intersection_merged_map.csv", header=TRUE)
colnames(map)[colnames(map) == "hgvs_pro"] ="base__achange"
colnames(map)[colnames(map) == "score"] ="VEP_map"
colnames(clinvar1)[colnames(clinvar1) == "hgvsp"] ="base__achange"
#match data to Cindy's format
AIREdata <- merge(map, clinvar1, by = "base__achange")
#change clinvar notes to script-compatible notes
AIREdata <- AIREdata %>%
  mutate(referenceSet = case_when(
    grepl("Positive", AIREdata$referenceSet) ~ "P/LP",
    grepl("Negative", AIREdata$referenceSet) ~ "B/LB",
    TRUE ~ "no known"
  ))
colnames(AIREdata)[colnames(AIREdata) == "referenceSet"] ="clinvar"
# Note about my input: 'base__achange' is equivalent of hgvsp with format p.Aaa000Aaa. 'clinvar' value is P/LP or B/LB (no VUS or conflicting). VEP_map is score 
withpos <- AIREdata %>%
  mutate(pos = as.numeric(str_extract(base__achange, "(?<=^p\\.[A-Za-z]{3})[0-9]+(?=[A-Za-z]{3}$)"))) %>% # allows 4 digit position numbers
  select(base__achange, clinvar, VEP_map, pos) #%>% # Change VEP
 #rename(VEP = VEP_map)
colnames(withpos)[colnames(withpos) == "VEP_map"] ="VEP"
threshold = 0.5 # CHANGE THRESHOLD depending on your VEP/map

# ground truth as binary (1 = P/LP, 0 = B/LB)
labeled <- withpos %>%
  mutate(
    truth = ifelse(clinvar == "P/LP", 1, 0),
    predicted_label = ifelse(VEP <= threshold, 1, 0)
  )

labeled <- labeled %>%
  mutate(
    prediction_type = case_when(
      truth == 1 & predicted_label == 1 ~ "Pathogenic Concordant",
      truth == 0 & predicted_label == 0 ~ "Benign Concordant",
      truth == 0 & predicted_label == 1 ~ "Benign with Score ≤ 0.5",
      truth == 1 & predicted_label == 0 ~ "Pathogenic with Score > 0.5"
    )
  )

#extract discordant variants
discordant_Var <- labeled[!grepl("Concordant", labeled$prediction_type),]
#save discordant variants with all information
#consistant columns
discordant_variants <- discordant_Var %>% 
  mutate(hgvs_pro = base__achange) %>%  # create hgvs_pro for joining
  select(hgvs_pro)
#scores for discordant var
discord_var_scores <- score_file_ew_types %>%
  semi_join(discordant_variants, by = "hgvs_pro")
write.csv(discord_var_scores, "discordantVarscores.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# lollipop colors
colors <- c("Pathogenic Concordant" = "red", "Benign Concordant" = "red", "Benign with Score ≤ 0.5" = "black", "Pathogenic with Score > 0.5" = "black")

# protein domain ranges
domain_data <- data.frame(
  domain = c("CARD", "Disordered", "SAND not disordered", "Disordered",
             "PHD1", "Disordered", "PHD2", "Disordered", "Cterm"),
  start = c(1, 106, 181, 234, 296, 348, 434, 489, 515),
  end   = c(105, 178, 233, 290, 343, 382, 475, 508, 545)
)

# where to place domain bars
domain_data$ymin <- 0.48
domain_data$ymax <- 0.52

domain_colors <- c(
  "CARD"               = "#FF6F61",  # coral red
  "Disordered"         = "#BDC3C7",  # light gray
  "SAND not disordered"= "#6C5B7B",  # muted violet
  "PHD1"               = "#1B998B",  # teal blue
  "PHD2"               = "#FFB400",  # golden yellow
  "Cterm"              = "#8D8741"   # olive green
)

# Lollipop plot
main_plot <- ggplot(labeled, aes(x = pos, y = VEP, color = prediction_type)) +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = pos, y = threshold, yend = VEP), size = 0.2) + 
  geom_point(size = 1, alpha = 0.6) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = colors) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.371, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.667, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = threshold, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.733, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.701, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.599, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.110, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.150,  linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.915,  linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1.565,  linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1.699,  linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_rect(
    data = domain_data,
    aes(xmin = start, xmax = end, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = domain),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = NA,
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = domain_colors) +  # <-- custom domain scheme
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Position",
    y = "Score",
    color = "Concordance", 
    fill = "AIRE Domains"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(min(labeled$VEP), max(labeled$VEP)))

print(main_plot)
ggsave(filename = "lollipop_AIRE.pdf", main_plot, device = pdf, width = 7, height =5)
```

```{r VUS_plot_overProtein, echo = FALSE}
#This script shows VUS scores and evidence strengths along the AIRE protein for Figure S16B
#pull random set of clinvar VUS variants for discussion question
# pull random set of clinvar VUS variants for discussion question
ClinVarSet <- read.csv("20250825_AIRE_missense_clinvar_result.csv")
VUS_set <- ClinVarSet[ClinVarSet$Germline.classification == "Uncertain significance",]
VUSvariants <- unique(VUS_set$Protein.change)
RandomSample <- sample(VUSvariants, 33)
write.csv(RandomSample, "randomVUSList.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# extract VUS score and evidence strengths that are non-undetermined
VUS_lollipop <- score_file_ew_types[which(
  score_file_ew_types$type == "VUS" &
  !score_file_ew_types$evidence_weight == "undetermined"),
]

threshold = 0.5

# protein domain ranges
domain_data <- data.frame(
  domain = c("CARD", "Disordered", "SAND not disordered", "Disordered",
             "PHD1", "Disordered", "PHD2", "Disordered", "Cterm"),
  start = c(1, 106, 181, 234, 296, 348, 434, 489, 515),
  end   = c(105, 178, 233, 290, 343, 382, 475, 508, 545)
)

# where to place domain bars (same placement as first script)
domain_data$ymin <- 0.48
domain_data$ymax <- 0.52

# domain colors
domain_colors <- c(
  "CARD"               = "#FF6F61",
  "Disordered"         = "#BDC3C7",
  "SAND not disordered"= "#6C5B7B",
  "PHD1"               = "#1B998B",
  "PHD2"               = "#FFB400",
  "Cterm"              = "#8D8741"
)

# lollipop plot (same style as first script)
VUSplot <- ggplot(VUS_lollipop, aes(x = pos, y = score)) +

  geom_segment(aes(xend = pos, y = threshold, yend = score), size = 0.2) +

  geom_point(size = 1, alpha = 0.6, color = "black") +

  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.371, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.667, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = threshold, linetype = "dotted", color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.733, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.701, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.599, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -0.110, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.150,  linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.915,  linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1.565,  linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1.699,  linetype = "dotted", color = "grey50") +

  geom_rect(
    data = domain_data,
    aes(xmin = start, xmax = end, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = domain),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = NA
  ) +

  scale_fill_manual(values = domain_colors) +

  theme_minimal() +

  labs(
    x = "Position",
    y = "Score",
    fill = "AIRE Domains"
  ) +

  coord_cartesian(
    xlim = c(1, 545),
    ylim = c(min(VUS_lollipop$score), max(VUS_lollipop$score))
  )

print(VUSplot)

ggsave("VUSDistributionplot.pdf", VUSplot, width = 7, height = 5)
```

```{r to_amino_acid_category, echo = FALSE}
#this chunk is the function to run the disorder vs not disorder by category (plots below)
analyze_data_TO <- function(data, title, width, height) {
  # Create the initial plot
  data <- data[data$aa_type_To != data$aa_type_From,]
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = aa_type_To, y = score)) + 
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(x = "to amino acid type", y = "variant functional score") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
  
  # Get all unique categories
  categories <- unique(data$aa_type_To)
  
  # Initialize an empty dataframe to store the results
  results <- data.frame(Category1 = character(), Category2 = character(), 
                        p.value = numeric(), estimate = numeric(), 
                        Median1 = numeric(), Median2 = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # List to store comparisons for stat_compare_means
  comparisons <- list()
  
  # Loop through each combination of two categories
  for(i in 1:(length(categories)-1)){
    for(j in (i+1):length(categories)){
      category1 <- categories[i]
      category2 <- categories[j]
      
      # Subset the data for the two categories
      category1_data <- data[data$aa_type_To == category1,]
      category2_data <- data[data$aa_type_To == category2,]
      
      # Perform the Wilcoxon test
      test_result <- wilcox.test(category1_data$score, category2_data$score, 
                                 mu=0, alt="two.sided", conf.int=T, conf.level = 0.95, 
                                 paired=F, correct=T)
      
      # Extract the p-value and estimate
      p_value <- test_result$p.value
      estimate <- test_result$estimate
      
      # Calculate medians
      median1 <- median(category1_data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
      median2 <- median(category2_data$score, na.rm = TRUE)
      
      # Add the results to the dataframe
      results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Category1 = category1, Category2 = category2, 
                                           p.value = p_value, estimate = estimate, 
                                           Median1 = median1, Median2 = median2))
    }
  }
  
  # Apply the Bonferroni correction
  results$p.value <- p.adjust(results$p.value, method = "bonferroni")
  
  # Filter only significant results
  sig_results <- results[results$p.value < 0.05,]
  
  # Write significant results to file
  write.csv(file = paste0(title, ".csv"), sig_results, row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Add significance bars to the plot for significant results
  if (nrow(sig_results) > 0) {
    sig_comparisons <- mapply(function(cat1, cat2) c(cat1, cat2), 
                              sig_results$Category1, sig_results$Category2, 
                              SIMPLIFY = FALSE)
    p <- p + stat_compare_means(comparisons = sig_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", 
                                label = "p.signif", p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
  }
  
  # Print the plot to PDF
  ggsave(p, device = "pdf", filename = paste0(title, ".pdf"), width = width, height = height)

  # Print the plot to the console
  print(p)
  
  # Print the medians
  cat("Medians of categories:\n")
  for(category in categories) {
    median_value <- median(data[data$aa_type_To == category,]$score, na.rm = TRUE)
    cat("Category:", category, "Median:", median_value, "\n")
  }
}
```

```{r disordered_regions_analysis}
#this chunk analyzes disordered regions by category for Figure S13 (only hydrophobic used) 
disorder <- map_processed[map_processed$pos %in% c(101:178, 234:290, 348:382, 489:508), ]
not_disordered <- map_processed[!(map_processed$pos %in% c(101:178, 234:290, 348:382, 489:508)), ]

analyze_data_TO_simple(not_disordered, "hydrophobic", "change_to_hydrophobic_not_disordered.pdf", "change_to_hydrophobic_not_disordered.csv")
analyze_data_TO_simple(disorder, "hydrophobic", "change_to_hydrophobic_disorder.pdf", "change_to_hydrophobic_disorder.csv")
analyze_data_TO_simple(not_disordered, "neg_charged", "change_to_negcharge_not_disordered.pdf", "change_to_negcharge_not_disordered.csv")
analyze_data_TO_simple(disorder, "neg_charged", "change_to_negcharge_disorder.pdf", "change_to_negcharge_disorder.csv")
analyze_data_TO_simple(not_disordered, "pos_charged", "change_to_poscharge_not_disordered.pdf", "change_to_poscharge_not_disordered.csv")
analyze_data_TO_simple(disorder, "pos_charged", "change_to_poscharge_disorder.pdf", "change_to_poscharge_disorder.csv")
analyze_data_TO_simple(not_disordered, "proline", "change_to_proline_not_disordered.pdf", "change_to_proline_not_disordered.csv")
analyze_data_TO_simple(disorder, "proline", "change_to_proline_disorder.pdf", "change_to_proline_disorder.csv")
```

```{r follow_up_variants, echo = FALSE}
#this chunk generates the barplots for follow up variants in Figure S14

data <- read.csv("2023Dec21_AIRE_FollowUp_Plot.csv")

#add data label type
data$variant_type <- c("control", "control", "control", "known LOF", 
                       "putative LOF", "putative LOF", "putative GOF", 
                       "putative GOF", "putative GOF")

data$Sample <- factor(data$Sample, levels = c('untransfected control','empty vector control','WT', 'R92W', 'P126V', 'P129R', 'A138M','P162H','P162Y'))

p <- ggplot(data, aes(x=Sample, y=average_Wtset, fill = variant_type)) + 
  geom_bar(stat="identity",
           position=position_dodge()) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=average_Wtset-sd_Wtset, ymax=average_Wtset+sd_Wtset), width=.2,
                position=position_dodge(.9)) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.05))) +
  ylab("Frequency to the right of top 1% of WT cells")


p + theme(text = element_text(size=16),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle=90, hjust=1))


attempt <- read.csv("20240628_percents.csv")

# Rename "mCh" to "empty_vector"
attempt <- attempt %>%
  mutate(Sample = ifelse(Sample == "mCh", "empty_vector", Sample))
#removed untransfected
attempt <- attempt[!grepl("UT", attempt$Sample),]

# Calculate SD and mean of each variant
test <- attempt %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_gatefreq = mean(frequency, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_gatefreq = ifelse(n() > 1, sd(frequency, na.rm = TRUE), NA),
    count = n()
  )

# Create a file to write the output
output_file <- file("output.csv", "w")

# Perform the t-test manually
for (i in 1:nrow(test)) {
  if (test$Sample[i]!= "WT" && test$Sample[i]!= "empty_vector") {
    # Perform the t-test manually between WT and current row
    wt_result <- t_test_manual(test$mean_gatefreq[test$Sample == "WT"], 
                               test$sd_gatefreq[test$Sample == "WT"], 
                               3, 
                               test$mean_gatefreq[i], 
                               test$sd_gatefreq[i], 
                               3)
    
    # Perform the t-test manually between empty_vector and current row
    ev_result <- t_test_manual(test$mean_gatefreq[test$Sample == "empty_vector"], 
                               test$sd_gatefreq[test$Sample == "empty_vector"], 
                               3, 
                               test$mean_gatefreq[i], 
                               test$sd_gatefreq[i], 
                               3)
    
    # Multiply the p-value by 2 to bonferroni correct for 2 tests per variant
    wt_result$p_value <- wt_result$p_value * 2
    ev_result$p_value <- ev_result$p_value * 2
    
    # Write the results to the output file
    writeLines(c(paste("Results for", test$Sample[i]), 
                 paste("WT: t-statistic =", wt_result$t_stat, ", p-value =", wt_result$p_value), 
                 paste("empty_vector: t-statistic =", ev_result$t_stat, ", p-value =", ev_result$p_value), 
                 ""), output_file)
  }
}

# Perform the t-test manually between empty_vector and WT
ev_wt_result <- t_test_manual(test$mean_gatefreq[test$Sample == "empty_vector"], 
                              test$sd_gatefreq[test$Sample == "empty_vector"], 
                              3, 
                              test$mean_gatefreq[test$Sample == "WT"], 
                              test$sd_gatefreq[test$Sample == "WT"], 
                              3)

# Multiply the p-value by 2 to bonferroni correct for 2 tests per variant
ev_wt_result$p_value <- ev_wt_result$p_value * 2

# Write the results to the output file
writeLines(c(paste("Results for empty_vector vs WT"), 
             paste("t-statistic =", ev_wt_result$t_stat, ", p-value =", ev_wt_result$p_value), 
             ""), output_file)

# Close the output file
close(output_file)

test$Sample <- factor(test$Sample, levels = c('empty_vector','WT', 'R92W', 'P126V', 'P129R', 'A138M','P162H','P162Y'))

test <- test %>%
  mutate(type = case_when(
    Sample == 'empty_vector' ~ 'empty_vector',
    Sample == 'WT' ~ 'WT',
    Sample == 'R92W' ~ 'known pathogenic',
    Sample %in% c('P126V', 'P129R') ~ 'new LOF',
    Sample %in% c('A138M', 'P162H', 'P162Y') ~ 'increased',
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))

# Create the plot
p <- ggplot(test, aes(x = Sample, y = mean_gatefreq, fill = type)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) + 
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_gatefreq - sd_gatefreq, ymax = mean_gatefreq + sd_gatefreq), width = 0.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) + 
  labs(x = "variant", y = "gate frequency") + 
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("empty_vector" = "gray", "WT" = "black", "known pathogenic" = "magenta", "new LOF" = "lightpink", "increased" = "lightgreen")) + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)

ggsave(filename = "followup_barplot.pdf", p, width = 8, height = 5)
print(p)

```

```{r dominant_negative_variants, fig.show='hold', echo=FALSE}
#This chunk makes the plots for dominant negative variants for Figure S17

refset <- read.csv("20250825_clinvar_refset_oneStar.csv")
domInheritance <- read.csv("20240624_inher_patterns.csv")
map <- read.csv("20251110_finalScoreswEvidenceWeights_test.csv")
map_filtered <- map[map$numberofBioReps==2,]

#change map notation to match dom inheritance
map_filtered$hgvs_pro <- mgsub(map_filtered$hgvs_pro, pattern = c("Ala", "Arg", "Asn", "Asp", "Cys", "Gln",
                                                "Glu", "Gly", "His", "Ile", "Leu", "Lys",
                                                "Met", "Phe", "Pro", "Ser",
                                                "Thr", "Trp", "Tyr", "Val", "=", "Ter"), 
                      replacement = c("A", "R", "N", "D", "C", "Q", "E", 
                                      "G", "H", "I", "L", "K", "M", "F", "P",
                                      "S", "T", "W", "Y", "V", "=", "*"))
#remove p. from notation
map_filtered$hgvs_pro <- str_replace(map_filtered$hgvs_pro, "p.", "")
colnames(map_filtered)[colnames(map_filtered) == "hgvs_pro"] <- "Variant"

#Change variant notation to match the dom inheritance amino acid change style
refset$hgvsp <- mgsub(refset$hgvsp, pattern = c("Ala", "Arg", "Asn", "Asp", "Cys", "Gln",
                                                        "Glu", "Gly", "His", "Ile", "Leu", "Lys",
                                                        "Met", "Phe", "Pro", "Ser", "Thr",
                                                        "Trp", "Tyr", "Val", "=", "Ter"), 
                          replacement = c("A", "R", "N", "D", "C", "Q", "E", 
                                          "G", "H", "I", "L", "K", "M", "F", "P",
                                          "S", "T", "W", "Y", "V", "=", "*"))
#remove p. from notation
refset$hgvsp <- str_replace(refset$hgvsp, "p.", "")

#Eliminate variants with dominant inheritance from reference set
#which ones overlap
domneg_inRefSet <- refset[(refset$hgvsp %in% domInheritance$Variant), ]
#remove these from refset
refset <- refset[!(refset$hgvsp %in% domInheritance$Variant), ]

#name refset as autosomal recessive or benign
refset$referenceSet <- ifelse(refset$referenceSet == "Negative", "Benign", "Autosomal Recessive")
#rename refset
colnames(refset)[colnames(refset) == "referenceSet"] <- "Inheritance"
colnames(refset)[colnames(refset) == "hgvsp"] <- "Variant"
#maintain only refset columns needed
refset <- refset[,c("Inheritance", "Variant")]

#combine sets
total <- rbind(refset,domInheritance)

#find position and label by domain
# prepare regular expression
regexp <- "[[:digit:]]+"
# process string
total$pos <- as.numeric(str_extract(total$Variant, regexp))

#label by position
total <- total %>%
  mutate(Domain = case_when(
    pos >= 1 & pos <= 105 ~ "CARD",
    pos >= 101 & pos <= 178 ~ "Disordered",
    pos >= 181 & pos <= 280 ~ "SAND",
    pos >= 280 & pos <= 290 ~ "Disordered",
    pos >= 296 & pos <= 343 ~ "PHD1",
    pos >= 348 & pos <= 382 ~ "Disordered",
    pos >= 434 & pos <= 475 ~ "PHD2",
    pos >= 489 & pos <= 503 ~ "Disordered",
    pos >= 515 & pos <= 545 ~ "C-term",
    TRUE ~ "no annotation"
  ))

# Filter domInheritance to exclude PHD1 domain
#find all rows that are DomNeg
total_domNeg <- total[total$Inheritance == "DomNeg",]
#label those in the PHD1 domain
total_domNeg_PHD1 <- total_domNeg[total_domNeg$Domain == "PHD1",]
#eliminate those in the PHD1 domain from total
total_domNeg <- total_domNeg[!total_domNeg$Domain == "PHD1",]
#change this to another category
total_domNeg$Inheritance <- "Dominant negative outside PHD1"
total_domNeg_PHD1$Inheritance <- "Dominant negative within PHD1"

#add this back to the total as a separate category
total <- rbind(total, total_domNeg, total_domNeg_PHD1)
#remove rows that have total DomNeg, double check that's duplicated
total <- total[!total$Inheritance=="DomNeg",]
nrow(total)
length(unique(total$Variant))

#merge with map_filtered scores
total_scores <- merge(total, map_filtered, by = "Variant")

#find medians
total_scores %>%
  group_by(Inheritance) %>%
  summarise(median_score = median(score))

# Draw boxplot by inheritance
byInheritance <- ggplot(total_scores, aes(x = Inheritance, y = score)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) + 
  labs(x = "variant category", y = "variant functional score", color = "domain")

# Test significance
inheritance_types <- unique(total_scores$Inheritance)

results <- sapply(inheritance_types, function(x) {
  sapply(inheritance_types, function(y) {
    if (x != y) {
      wilcox.test(score ~ Inheritance, data = total_scores %>% 
                    filter(Inheritance %in% c(x, y)), 
                  mu = 0, alt = "two.sided", conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.95, 
                 correct = TRUE)$p.value
    } else {
      NA
    }
  })
})

results <- as.data.frame(results)
rownames(results) <- inheritance_types
colnames(results) <- inheritance_types

# Apply the Bonferroni correction to the p-values
results[] <- p.adjust(as.matrix(results), method = "bonferroni")

# Print the corrected results
print(results)

# Create a data frame for significant results
sig_results <- results %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  rownames_to_column("Category1") %>%
  pivot_longer(-Category1, names_to = "Category2", values_to = "p_value") %>%
  filter(!is.na(p_value) & p_value < 0.05)

# Ensure unique comparisons
sig_results <- sig_results %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(comparison = paste(sort(c(Category1, Category2)), collapse = "-")) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  distinct(comparison, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(Category1, Category2, p_value)

sig_comparisons <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(sig_results)), function(i) {
  c(sig_results$Category1[i], sig_results$Category2[i])
})

# Add significance bars to the plot for significant results
if (nrow(sig_results) > 0) {
  byInheritance <- byInheritance + stat_compare_means(comparisons = sig_comparisons, 
                                                      method = "wilcox.test", 
                                                      label = "p.signif", 
                                                      p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
}

# Display the plot
print(byInheritance)
ggsave(filename = "by_inheritance.pdf", byInheritance, width = 4, height =4)

# Define your custom color palette
domain_colors <- c(
  "CARD"               = "#FF6F61",  # coral red
  "Disordered"         = "#BDC3C7",  # light gray
  "SAND"               = "#6C5B7B",  # muted violet
  "PHD1"               = "#1B998B",  # teal blue
  "PHD2"               = "#FFB400",  # golden yellow
  "C-term"            = "#8D8741",   # olive green
  "no annotation"      = "#3C91E6" #blue
)

# Plot
byVariant_noPHD1 <- ggplot(total_scores, aes(x = Inheritance, y = score, color = Domain)) + 
geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.05), size = 2) + 
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Variant),
    nudge_x = 0.15,        # slight horizontal offset
    nudge_y = 0.05,        # slight vertical offset
    angle = 30,            # rotate labels a bit
    size = 2,
    hjust = 0              # align text to the left of the point
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = domain_colors) +  # Apply custom colors
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) + 
  labs(
    x = "Variant category", 
    y = "Variant functional score", 
    color = "Domain"
  )

# Show plot
print(byVariant_noPHD1)

# Save plot
ggsave(
  filename = "by_inheritance_wVariants.pdf", 
  plot = byVariant_noPHD1, 
  width = 5, 
  height = 4
)

######Note, the DN variants reported in ClinVar were G228W, C311Y and P326L
```

```{r CAM_genopheno, echo = FALSE}
#This script analyzes and makes figures for genotype to phenotype correlations for Figure 4A, Figure 4B, and Figure S18
process_patient_data <- function(with_PHD1 = TRUE) {
  # Read input files
  patients <- read.csv("genphen_readable.csv")
  map <- read.csv("20231018_WholeMap.csv")
  
  # Remove all brackets from dataset
  patients <- data.frame(apply(patients, 2, function(x) gsub("[(&)]", "", x)))
  colnames(patients)[colnames(patients) == "allele.1.protein"] <- "hgvs_pro"
  
  # Merge allele 1 with scores
  merge1 <- merge(patients, map, by = "hgvs_pro", all.x = TRUE)
  colnames(merge1)[colnames(merge1) == "score"] <- "allele_1_score"
  colnames(merge1)[colnames(merge1) == "se"] <- "allele_1_se"
  
  # Merge allele 2 with scores
  colnames(merge1)[colnames(merge1) == "hgvs_pro"] <- "allele.1.protein"
  colnames(merge1)[colnames(merge1) == "allele.2.protein"] <- "hgvs_pro"
  merge2 <- merge(merge1, map, by = "hgvs_pro", all.x = TRUE)
  colnames(merge2)[colnames(merge2) == "score"] <- "allele_2_score"
  colnames(merge2)[colnames(merge2) == "se"] <- "allele_2_se"
  colnames(merge2)[colnames(merge2) == "hgvs_pro"] <- "allele.2.protein"
  
  # Fill in scores for splice variants, WT, and ter/alamut
  merge2$allele_2_score <- ifelse(grepl("[-+]", merge2$allele.2.cDNA), 0, merge2$allele_2_score)
  merge2$allele_2_score <- ifelse(merge2$allele.2.cDNA == "", 1, merge2$allele_2_score)
  merge2$allele_2_score <- ifelse(grepl("Ter|alamut", merge2$allele.2.protein), 0, merge2$allele_2_score)
  
  # Remove patients missing scores
  merge2 <- merge2[!(is.na(merge2$allele_1_score) | is.na(merge2$allele_2_score)), ]
  
  # Calculate total scores
  merge2$tot_score <- rowSums(merge2[, c("allele_1_score", "allele_2_score")], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Count phenotypes per patient
  merge2 <- merge2 %>% 
    mutate(count = lengths(strsplit(Phenotypes, ","))) %>%
    mutate(Phenotypes = gsub("†", "", Phenotypes))
  
  # Handle PHD1 filtering if needed
  if (!with_PHD1) {
    regexp <- "[[:digit:]]+"
    merge2$pos1 <- as.numeric(str_extract(merge2$allele.1.protein, regexp))
    merge2$pos2 <- as.numeric(str_extract(merge2$allele.2.protein, regexp))
    merge2$pos2[grepl("Ter", merge2$allele.2.protein)] <- NA
    merge2 <- merge2[!(merge2$pos1 >= 296 & merge2$pos1 <= 343), ]
    merge2 <- merge2[is.na(merge2$pos2) | !(merge2$pos2 >= 296 & merge2$pos2 <= 343), ]
  }
  
  # Group patients into dyad vs not
  merge2 <- merge2 %>%
    mutate(label = case_when(
      (grepl("Add", Phenotypes) & grepl("HP", Phenotypes)) |
        (grepl("Add", Phenotypes) & grepl("CMC", Phenotypes)) |
        (grepl("CMC", Phenotypes) & grepl("HP", Phenotypes)) ~ "dyad",
      TRUE ~ "else"
    ))
  
 # Calculate and print medians for dyad and else groups
  median_dyad <- median(merge2$tot_score[merge2$label == "dyad"], na.rm = TRUE)
  median_else <- median(merge2$tot_score[merge2$label == "else"], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  cat("Median CAM score for dyad group:", median_dyad, "\n")
  cat("Median CAM score for else group:", median_else, "\n")

  # Save patients with dyad and without dyad
  condition <- if (with_PHD1) "with_PHD1" else "noPHD1"
  write.csv(merge2[merge2$label == "dyad", ], 
            file = paste0("patients_with_dyad_", condition, ".csv"), 
            row.names = FALSE)
  write.csv(merge2[merge2$label == "else", ], 
            file = paste0("patients_without_dyad_", condition, ".csv"), 
            row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Calculate correlation
  cor_result <- cor.test(merge2$tot_score, merge2$count)
  
  # Generate scatterplot
  condition <- if (with_PHD1) "with_PHD1" else "noPHD1"
  scatterplot <- ggplot(merge2, aes(x = tot_score, y = count)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
    labs(x = "CAM score",
         y = "Number of APS-1 symptoms") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
    annotate("text", x = min(merge2$tot_score), 
             y = max(merge2$count), 
             label = paste("R =", round(cor_result$estimate, 2), ", p =", format.pval(cor_result$p.value, digits = 2)),
             hjust = 0, vjust = 1)
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0("CAM_SympNumber_", condition, ".pdf"), scatterplot, width = 7, height = 5)
  
  # Generate boxplot for dyad vs else
  boxplot <- ggplot(data = merge2, aes(label, tot_score)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(x = "Patient symptom group",
         y = "CAM score") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 15)
  
  # Test significance and add significance bars if needed
  results <- wilcox.test(tot_score ~ label, data = merge2, 
                         mu = 0, alt = "two.sided", conf.int = TRUE, conf.level = 0.95, 
                         correct = TRUE)$p.value
  results <- p.adjust(results, method = "bonferroni")
  print(results)
  if (results < 0.05) {
    boxplot <- boxplot + stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("dyad", "else")), 
                                            method = "wilcox.test", 
                                            label = "p.signif", 
                                            p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
  }
  
  ggsave(filename = paste0("all_dyad_vs_else_", condition, ".pdf"), boxplot, width = 4, height = 4)
  
  return(list(scatterplot = scatterplot, boxplot = boxplot))
}

#Process geno-pheno
# With PHD1
process_patient_data(with_PHD1 = TRUE)

# Without PHD1
process_patient_data(with_PHD1 = FALSE)

#read all groups of patients
  all_dyad_patients <- read.csv("patients_with_dyad_with_PHD1.csv")
  all_else_patients <- read.csv("patients_without_dyad_with_PHD1.csv")
  all_dyad_patients_noPHD1 <- read.csv("patients_with_dyad_noPHD1.csv")
  all_else_patients_noPHD1 <- read.csv("patients_without_dyad_noPHD1.csv")
  
  #n all patients
  print(nrow(all_dyad_patients) + nrow(all_else_patients))
  #n only those with no phd1
  print(nrow(all_dyad_patients_noPHD1) + nrow(all_else_patients_noPHD1))
  
  print(nrow(all_dyad_patients_noPHD1))
  print(nrow(all_else_patients_noPHD1))
  print(nrow(all_dyad_patients))
  print(nrow(all_else_patients))
  
#label as what kind of genotype they have
  #this is ok because there are no nonsense heterozygotes in dataset
  all_dyad_patients$genotype <- ifelse(all_dyad_patients$allele.1.protein == all_dyad_patients$allele.2.protein, "homozygous missense", "")
  all_dyad_patients$genotype[all_dyad_patients$genotype == "" &
                           (grepl("Ter", all_dyad_patients$allele.1.protein) |
                            grepl("Ter", all_dyad_patients$allele.2.protein))] <-
  "nonsense heterozygous"
  
   #same for all Else
  all_else_patients$genotype <- ifelse(all_else_patients$allele.1.protein == all_else_patients$allele.2.protein, "homozygous missense", "")
  all_else_patients$genotype[all_else_patients$genotype == "" &
                           (grepl("Ter", all_else_patients$allele.1.protein) |
                            grepl("Ter", all_else_patients$allele.2.protein))] <-
  "nonsense heterozygous"
  
  #merge to get ALL 
  all_patients <- rbind(all_dyad_patients, all_else_patients)
  table(all_patients$genotype)
  sum(table(all_patients$genotype))
  #there are 5 in the no annotation bin that should be in the nonsense heterozygos bin. Because they have no allele 1. one is mising because they have no score
```

```{r test_maps_confirmCorrect, echo = FALSE}
######make sure that processed and non-processed maps are identical
###This is the mavedb map with all processing
map_pr_option2 <- read.csv("20251110_finalScoreswEvidenceWeights_test.csv")
#input map that was used previously
map_original <- read.csv("20231018_WholeMap.csv")
#these should match
print(median(map_original$score))
#these should also match
print(median(map_pr_option2$se))
print(median(map_original$se))
```

```{r reclassfication, echo = FALSE}
#analysis for numbers in manuscript for how many variants could be reclassified based on functional evidence
reclassed_table <- read.csv("ClinVar_reclassifications_EiBr_271025.csv")
#extract variant
reclassed_table$hgvs_pro <- sub(",.*", "", reclassed_table[,1])
#count up results for all VUS
VUSinitial <- reclassed_table[reclassed_table$ClinVar.Class == "VUS",]
#frequency of each Final.class
vus_counts <- table(VUSinitial$Final.class)
#convert counts to percentages
vus_percent <- prop.table(vus_counts) * 100
#show percentage of each
classes_of_VUS_reclass <- data.frame(
  Final.class = names(vus_counts),
  Count = as.vector(vus_counts),
  Percent = round(as.vector(vus_percent), 2)
)

print(classes_of_VUS_reclass)

#count up results for all LP/P
Pinitial <- reclassed_table[reclassed_table$ClinVar.Class == "P"|reclassed_table$ClinVar.Class == "LP"|reclassed_table$ClinVar.Class == "P/LP",]
#frequency of each Final.class
P_counts <- table(Pinitial$Final.class)
#convert counts to percentages
P_percent <- prop.table(P_counts) * 100
#show percentage of each
classes_of_P_reclass <- data.frame(
  Final.class = names(P_counts),
  Count = as.vector(P_counts),
  Percent = round(as.vector(P_percent), 2)
)

print(classes_of_P_reclass)

#Processing conflicts
conflict_initial <- reclassed_table[grepl("Conflicting", reclassed_table$ClinVar.Class),]
#number with reclass
table(conflict_initial$Final.class)


#seems to be one missing from VUS after reclass, and one P, find out which
variant_evidence_weight_table <- read.csv("20251110_finalScoreswEvidenceWeights_test.csv")
variant_evidence_weight_table_P <- variant_evidence_weight_table[grepl("P",variant_evidence_weight_table$type) & !grepl("undetermined", variant_evidence_weight_table$evidence_weight),]
not_in_Pinitial <- variant_evidence_weight_table_P$hgvs_pro[
  !(variant_evidence_weight_table_P$hgvs_pro %in% Pinitial$hgvs_pro)
]

#why are three missing from evidence classifications for VUS
variant_evidence_weight_table_VUS <- variant_evidence_weight_table[grepl("VUS",variant_evidence_weight_table$type) & !grepl("undetermined", variant_evidence_weight_table$evidence_weight),]
variant_evidence_weight_table_VUS <- variant_evidence_weight_table_VUS[!grepl("p.Thr391Asn|p.Leu428Val", variant_evidence_weight_table_VUS$hgvs_pro),]
not_in_VUSinitial <- variant_evidence_weight_table_VUS$hgvs_pro[
  !(variant_evidence_weight_table_VUS$hgvs_pro %in% VUSinitial$hgvs_pro)
]

length(unique(variant_evidence_weight_table_VUS$hgvs_pro))
nrow(variant_evidence_weight_table_VUS)

```

```{r countThings_forFinalNumbers_inManuscript, echo = FALSE}
#This section answers questions in the manuscript text
#Question: How many missense variants have scores?
map_input <- read.csv("20251110_finalScoreswEvidenceWeights_test.csv", header=TRUE)
#extract only missense variants
map_input_missense <- map_input[!(grepl("Ter", map_input$hgvs_pro)|grepl("=", map_input$hgvs_pro)),]
print(paste("The number of total missense variants with scores is", nrow(map_input_missense)))

#Question: how many SNVs captured? total SNVs is called missense_df above
map_input_missense_SNVs <- map_input_missense[map_input_missense$SNVaccessible == "yes",]
nrow(map_input_missense_SNVs)
nrow(missense_df)
#percent of SNVs that have scores given by maps
print(nrow(map_input_missense_SNVs)/nrow(missense_df))

#Question: How many SNV-accessible missense variants have non-undetermined evidence strength?
SNVaccesswEvidence <- map_input_missense[
  !is.na(map_input_missense$evidence_weight) & 
  map_input_missense$evidence_weight != "undetermined" &
    map_input_missense$SNVaccessible == "yes",
]
nrow(SNVaccesswEvidence)/nrow(missense_df)
#how many of those variants are VUS?
VUS_SNVaccess <- SNVaccesswEvidence[which(SNVaccesswEvidence$ClinVarClass == "VUS"),]
nrow(VUS_SNVaccess)/345

#Question: How many current VUS have proactive evidence?
VUS_table <- map_input_missense[which(map_input_missense$ClinVarClass == "VUS"),]
#number of VUS with non-undetermined evidence
VUSevidence <- sum(table(VUS_table$evidence_weight)[names(table(VUS_table$evidence_weight)) != "undetermined"])
#total number of VUS in reference set
clinvar <- read.csv("20250825_AIRE_missense_clinvar_result.csv")
totalVUS <- sum(clinvar$Germline.classification == "Uncertain significance")
VUSevidence/totalVUS
#Question: how many missense variants in ClinVar are VUS? 
clinvar_missense <- clinvar[grepl("missense variant", clinvar$Molecular.consequence),] 
tbl <- table(clinvar_missense$Germline.classification)
total_variants <- sum(tbl)
vus_count <-  tbl["Uncertain significance"]
vus_prop <- vus_count / total_variants
vus_prop
#Question: how many reference set variants had evidence towards the correct evidence direction? 
#lollipop data is called labeled
labeled <- labeled %>%
  mutate(PHD1_variant = ifelse(pos >= 296 & pos <= 343, "Yes", "No"))
totalRefVarwithTwoBioRep <- sum(table(labeled$clinvar,labeled$prediction_type))
sumConcordantincludePHD1 <- sum(table(labeled$clinvar,labeled$prediction_type)[, c("Benign Concordant", "Pathogenic Concordant")])
sumConcordantincludePHD1/totalRefVarwithTwoBioRep

totalRefVarwithTwoBioRep_noPHD1 <- sum(table(labeled$clinvar[labeled$PHD1_variant == "No"],
      labeled$prediction_type[labeled$PHD1_variant == "No"]))
sumConcordant_noPHD1 <- sum(table(labeled$clinvar[labeled$PHD1_variant == "No"],
      labeled$prediction_type[labeled$PHD1_variant == "No"])[, c("Benign Concordant", "Pathogenic Concordant")])
sumConcordant_noPHD1/totalRefVarwithTwoBioRep_noPHD1

#Question: how many missense AIRE variants that currently do not have a clinical classification got evidence? and how many have a caution flag?
varCount <- map_input_missense[
  is.na(map_input_missense$ClinVarClass) & 
  !is.na(map_input_missense$evidence_weight) & 
  map_input_missense$evidence_weight != "undetermined",
]

#totalvar with non undetermined evidence weight
sum(table(varCount$evidence_weight))
table(varCount$evidenceWeightConfidence)

#Question: what are the counts and numbers of variants in classifications which were already noted as benign or pathogenic or vus? 
benignVar <- map_input_missense[grepl("B/LB", map_input_missense$ClinVarClass),]
PathVar <- map_input_missense[grepl("P/LP", map_input_missense$ClinVarClass),]
ConflVar <- map_input_missense[grepl("Confli", map_input_missense$ClinVarClass),]
VUSVarwevidence <- map_input_missense[
  grepl("^VUS$", map_input_missense$ClinVarClass) &
  !(grepl("undetermined", map_input_missense$evidence_weight)),
]
table(VUSVarwevidence$FinalClass)
sum(table(VUSVarwevidence$FinalClass))
table(VUSVarwevidence$Reclass)

#Question: what is the domain breakdown of the VUS with evidence?
#annotate VUS by domain
VUSVarwevidence <- VUSVarwevidence %>%
  mutate(Domain = case_when(
    pos >= 1 & pos <= 105 ~ "CARD",
    pos >= 181 & pos <= 280 ~ "SAND",
    pos >= 296 & pos <= 343 ~ "PHD1",
    pos >= 434 & pos <= 475 ~ "PHD2",
    pos >= 515 & pos <= 545 ~ "C-term"
  ))
#patho evidence in VUS
pathoEvVUS <- VUSVarwevidence[grepl("patho", VUSVarwevidence$evidence_weight),]
#breakdown by domain
table(pathoEvVUS$Domain)

#Question: what is the percent of VUS with confident classifications?
#general number of VUS reclass
table(VUSVarwevidence$Reclass)
#filter: reclass = "Yes" and no CAUTION
VUS_yes_noCaution <- VUSVarwevidence %>%
  filter(
    Reclass == "Yes",
    is.na(evidenceWeightConfidence) | evidenceWeightConfidence != "CAUTION"
  )
percent_yes_noCaution <- VUS_yes_noCaution %>%
  summarise(percent = n() / nrow(VUSVarwevidence) * 100) %>%
  pull(percent)
percent_yes_noCaution
nrow(VUS_yes_noCaution)

#Question: Find damaging positions of the SAND domain in disordered section?
map_positional_SAND <- map_positional[map_positional$from_aa >= 234 & map_positional$from_aa <= 280,]

#Question: How many missense variants score >1? 
map_input_missense_over1 <- map_input_missense[map_input_missense$score>1,]
nrow(map_input_missense_over1)

###question: how many missense variants above 1 are SNV-accessible?
all_SNV_df_missense <- all_SNV_df[!(grepl("Ter", all_SNV_df$variant)|grepl("=", all_SNV_df$variant)),]
SNV_accessible_hyper <- intersect(map_input_missense_over1$hgvs_pro, all_SNV_df_missense$variant)
paste("Percent SNV-accessible hyper variants:", length(SNV_accessible_hyper)/nrow(all_SNV_df_missense)*100)

#Question: Did the SNV-accessible variants score better than the non-SNV accessible missense variants? 
bySNVAccessible <- ggplot(map_input_missense, aes(x = SNVaccessible, y = score)) + 
  geom_boxplot() + 
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) + 
  labs(x = "SNV accessibility", y = "variant functional score")

# test significance
snv_test <- wilcox.test(score ~ SNVaccessible, data = map_input_missense,
                        mu = 0, alt = "two.sided", conf.int = TRUE, 
                        conf.level = 0.95, correct = TRUE)
print(snv_test)

# Add significance to plot
bySNVAccessible <- bySNVAccessible + 
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = list(c("yes", "no")), 
                     method = "wilcox.test", 
                     label = "p.signif")

print(bySNVAccessible)
ggsave(filename = "by_snv_accessible.pdf", bySNVAccessible, width = 4, height = 5)
```

```{#r UKBB_analysis}
#but script used for analysis is below without associated data
#This script generates Figure 4C and Figure 4D

#load packages
library(ggVennDiagram)
library(stringr)
library(data.table)
library(mgsub)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)

####Prepare datasets and assign variables. Nothing needs to be modified past this section except see comp predictors below,
#all files will save with date and frequency######
#assign allele frequency of variants allowed in, for file names
freq <- 0.0001
#date for file names
date <- "20251102_predOwn_LR"
#score thresholds based on LLR annotations
threshold <- c("0.371", "0.667") # Adjust as needed   
#include PHD1? Make this equal to "" if including so that the file names save correctly. "no_PHD1" if not.
#this will add to file names as well
PHD1 <- ""

#Load ICD10 codes and symptoms
icd10_simple <- read.csv("phenotype_codes.csv", header = TRUE)

#order of icd10_codes
f <- factor(icd10_simple$symptom)
#do the icd10 codes have tiers? if no, put "" else do tiered_phenotypes
tier = "tiered_phenotypes"

#load score set
scores_raw <- read.csv("20231018_WholeMap.csv", header = TRUE)
#load in the UKBB data (called UKBB later)

#attach category labels to test set based on LLR thresholds
scores_LLR_labelled <- scores_raw %>%
  mutate(evidence_weight = case_when(
    score <= -0.733 ~ "undetermined",
    score > -0.733 & score <= -0.701 ~ "patho.support",
    score > -0.701 & score <= -0.599 ~ "patho.moderate",
    score > -0.599 & score <= -0.110 ~ "patho.strong",
    score > -0.110 & score <= 0.150 ~ "patho.moderate",
    score > 0.150 & score <= 0.371 ~ "patho.support",    
    score > 0.371 & score <= 0.667 ~ "undetermined",
    score > 0.667 & score <= 0.915 ~ "benign.support",
    score > 0.915 & score <= 1.565 ~ "benign.strong",
    score > 1.565 & score <= 1.699 ~ "benign.support",
    score > 1.699 ~"undetermined"
  ))

#label score categories with threshold names
scores_LLR_labelled$score_category <- "undetermined"
scores_LLR_labelled$score_category[scores_LLR_labelled$score <= threshold[1]] <- "low"

#####Process datasets#####
#Score_set#
#Change colname for score set
names(scores_LLR_labelled)[names(scores_LLR_labelled) == 'hgvs_pro'] <- 'AA_change'
#Change variant notation to match the UKBB amino acid change style
scores_LLR_labelled$AA_change <- mgsub(scores_LLR_labelled$AA_change, pattern = c("Ala", "Arg", "Asn", "Asp", "Cys", "Gln",
                                                                                  "Glu", "Gly", "His", "Ile", "Leu", "Lys",
                                                                                  "Met", "Phe", "Pro", "Pyl", "Ser", "Sec",
                                                                                  "Thr", "Trp", "Tyr", "Val", "=", "Ter"),
                                       replacement = c("A", "R", "N", "D", "C", "Q", "E",
                                                       "G", "H", "I", "L", "K", "M", "F", "P",
                                                       "O", "S", "U", "T", "W", "Y", "V", "", "*"))
#remove p. from notation
scores_LLR_labelled$AA_change <- str_replace(scores_LLR_labelled$AA_change, "p.", "")

#add column to scores to indicate if in PHD1 domain
# prepare regular expression
regexp <- "[[:digit:]]+"
# process string
scores_LLR_labelled$pos <- as.numeric(str_extract(scores_LLR_labelled$AA_change, regexp))
if (PHD1 == "no_PHD1") {
  # remove PHD1 domain
  scores_LLR_labelled <- scores_LLR_labelled[!(scores_LLR_labelled$pos >= 296 & scores_LLR_labelled$pos <= 343),] }

#UKBB#
#remove unneeded columns in UKBB
UKBB <- UKBB %>%
  select(-c(Feature, Feature_type, chr, pos, cDNA_position, CDS_position, ref, alt, Codons))
#Add AA change column
UKBB$AA_change <- paste(substr(UKBB$Amino_acids, 1, 1),
                        UKBB$Protein_position, substr(UKBB$Amino_acids, 3, 3), sep = "")

####Eliminate participants with low-frequency missense variants without map scores from all groups####
#Save this set to see what removed
#merge the datasets to label the variants with corresponding scores. Keep everything in UKBB even if no score (all.x)
UKBB_labelled <- merge(UKBB, scores_LLR_labelled, by = "AA_change", all.x = TRUE)

##### Process AM #####
#read in alphamissense scores and assign low-scoring variant category and high-scoring variant category
AM <- read.csv("20240122_AM_maveDB.csv")
names(AM)[names(AM) == 'am_class'] <- 'class'
names(AM)[names(AM) == 'hgvs_pro'] <- 'AA_change'
#convert the hgvs pro variants to amino acid code
AM$AA_change <- mgsub(AM$AA_change, pattern = c("Ala", "Arg", "Asn", "Asp", "Cys", "Gln",
                                                "Glu", "Gly", "His", "Ile", "Leu", "Lys",
                                                "Met", "Phe", "Pro", "Pyl", "Ser", "Sec",
                                                "Thr", "Trp", "Tyr", "Val", "Ter", "="),
                      replacement = c("A", "R", "N", "D", "C", "Q", "E",
                                      "G", "H", "I", "L", "K", "M", "F", "P",
                                      "O", "S", "U", "T", "W", "Y", "V", "*", "="))
#remove p.
AM$AA_change <- sub("p.", "", AM$AA_change)
#remove unneeded columns
AM <- AM %>%
  select(-c(uniprot_id))
names(AM)[names(AM) == 'score'] <- 'comp_score'
#Extract "pathogenic" AM variants
AM_damaging <- AM[AM$class=="pathogenic",]
names(AM_damaging)[names(AM_damaging) == 'score'] <- 'comp_score'

##### Process REVEL #####
REVEL <- read.table("dxigbB5erc0diVQG.txt", header= TRUE)
REVEL <- REVEL[REVEL$Gene == "ENSG00000160224",]  
REVEL <- REVEL[grepl("missense_variant", REVEL$Consequence), ]
names(REVEL)[names(REVEL) == 'REVEL'] <- 'REVEL_score'

#add AA_change column
REVEL$AA_change <- paste(substr(REVEL$Amino_acids, 1, 1),
                         REVEL$Protein_position, substr(REVEL$Amino_acids, 3, 3), sep = "")
#only take the important columns for revel
REVEL <- REVEL %>%
  select(c(AA_change, REVEL_score))
#rename cols to match
names(REVEL)[names(REVEL) == 'REVEL_score'] <- 'comp_score'
#add revel score class > 0.5
REVEL <- REVEL %>%
  mutate(class = case_when(
    comp_score > 0.5 ~ "damaging"
  ))
#remove duplicate rows that happen because different datasets combined into the revel set
REVEL <- REVEL[!duplicated(REVEL$AA_change), ]
#extract test score class (damaging)
REVEL_damaging <- REVEL[which(REVEL$class == "damaging"),]

#####Find out how much variant overlap is in the set and are called damaging#####
#extract low-scoring missense variants, no stops
map_low_scores <- scores_LLR_labelled[scores_LLR_labelled$score_category == "low" & !grepl("[*]", scores_LLR_labelled$AA_change),]
x <- list(map = map_low_scores$AA_change, alphaMissense = AM_damaging$AA_change, REVEL = REVEL_damaging$AA_change)
plot <- ggVennDiagram(x) + scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F4FAFE", high = "#4981BF")
# save the plot to a PDF file
pdf(paste(date, PHD1, "low_scores_venn_unfiltered_noelimlowmap.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 6, height = 5)
print(plot)
dev.off()

#####Prepare control set: extract known damaging from UKBB_labelled set and eliminate those participants from main set#####
damaging_collect <- UKBB_labelled[grepl("frameshift_variant", UKBB_labelled$Consequence)|
                                    grepl("splice_region_variant", UKBB_labelled$Consequence)|
                                    grepl("inframe_insertion", UKBB_labelled$Consequence)|
                                    grepl("start_lost", UKBB_labelled$Consequence)|
                                    grepl("stop_gained", UKBB_labelled$Consequence),]
#only include participants once in damaging set
damaging_collect <- damaging_collect[!duplicated(damaging_collect$eid), ]
#remove all people in entire set that have a damaging variant
UKBB_labelled <- UKBB_labelled[!UKBB_labelled$eid %in% unique(damaging_collect$eid),]
UKBB_labelled_missense <- UKBB_labelled[UKBB_labelled$Consequence == "missense_variant",]

#label UKBB with computational predictor scores
REVEL_UKBB_labelled <- merge(UKBB_labelled_missense, REVEL_damaging, by = "AA_change")
AM_UKBB_labelled <- merge(UKBB_labelled_missense, AM_damaging, by = "AA_change")

test_set <- UKBB_labelled_missense[UKBB_labelled_missense$score_category == "low",]

#####Prepare test set######
test_sets <- list(test_set, AM_UKBB_labelled, REVEL_UKBB_labelled)
# filter each set for lower than frequency, but allow in no AF given by gnomAD
filtered_test_sets <- lapply(test_sets, function(df) {
  df[df$gnomAD_AF < freq | is.na(df$gnomAD_AF), ]
})

# Access the filtered data frames using their indices
test_set <- filtered_test_sets[[1]]
AM_test_set <- filtered_test_sets[[2]]
REVEL_test_set <- filtered_test_sets[[3]]

#extract the control set: those that are not in the test set
filtered_control_sets <- lapply(test_sets, function(df) {
  UKBB_labelled[!UKBB_labelled$eid %in% unique(df$eid),]
})
control_set <- filtered_control_sets[[1]]
AM_control_set <- filtered_control_sets[[2]]
REVEL_control_set <- filtered_control_sets[[3]]

#remove duplicate eids in the control set (this maintains each participant once)
control_set <- control_set[!duplicated(control_set$eid),]
AM_control_set <- AM_control_set[!duplicated(AM_control_set$eid),]
REVEL_control_set <- REVEL_control_set[!duplicated(REVEL_control_set$eid),]

#are there people with more than one variant in the test set?
#if yes, only keep the lowest-scoring variant
if (nrow(test_set) != length(unique(test_set$eid))) {
  test_set <- test_set %>%
    group_by(eid) %>%
    slice_min(score, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>% # keeps only the row with min score
    ungroup()
}
if (nrow(AM_test_set) != length(unique(AM_test_set$eid))) {
  AM_test_set <- AM_test_set %>% group_by(eid) %>% slice(which.min(comp_score))
}
if (nrow(REVEL_test_set) != length(unique(REVEL_test_set$eid))) {
  REVEL_test_set <- REVEL_test_set %>% group_by(eid) %>% slice(which.min(comp_score))
}

#combine the test sets with the damaging_collect sets, but need to make sure they have the same colnames
#match column names
common_cols <- intersect(names(REVEL_test_set), names(damaging_collect))
# Select the common columns from each data frame
test_set <- test_set[common_cols]
damaging_collect <- damaging_collect[common_cols]
AM_test_set <- AM_test_set[common_cols]
REVEL_test_set <- REVEL_test_set[common_cols]

#of these sets we want to compare these groups to the non-damaging group
#bind the damaging with the test set scoring variants
test_set_w_damaging <- rbind(test_set, damaging_collect)
AM_w_damaging <- rbind(AM_test_set, damaging_collect)
REVEL_w_damaging <- rbind(REVEL_test_set, damaging_collect)

#combine all sets and unique the eids
All_w_damaging <- rbind(test_set, AM_test_set, REVEL_test_set, damaging_collect)
#Remove duplicate eids
All_w_damaging <- All_w_damaging[!duplicated(All_w_damaging$eid),]
#Prepare control set for everything other than all with damaging
All_w_damaging_control <- UKBB_labelled[!UKBB_labelled$eid %in% All_w_damaging$eid,]
All_w_damaging_control <- All_w_damaging_control[!duplicated(All_w_damaging_control$eid),]

conditions_to_test <- list(damaging_collect, test_set_w_damaging, AM_w_damaging, REVEL_w_damaging, All_w_damaging)
names(conditions_to_test) = c("cLOF", "cLOF or damaging map score",
                              "cLOF or damaging AM score", "cLOF or damaging REVEL score", "cLOF or damaging map, AM or REVEL score")
control_sets <- list(control_set, control_set, AM_control_set, REVEL_control_set, All_w_damaging_control)
names(control_sets) = c("control set", "control set", "AM control set", "REVEL control set", "All control")

new_row <- function(condition, icd_code) {
  data.frame(condition = names(conditions_to_test)[condition],
             symptom = icd10_simple$symptom[icd10_simple$ICD10code == icd_code],
             phenotype = icd_code,
             phenotype_tier = icd10_simple$tier[icd10_simple$ICD10code == icd_code],
             obs_category_w_symptom = freq_in_category,
             obs_category_wo_symptom = not_in_category,
             obs_control_w_symptom = in_control,
             obs_control_wo_symptom = not_in_control,
             OR = OR,
             SE_OR = SE_OR,
             UppCI = UppCI,
             LowCI = LowCI,
             chi_p = chi_p,
             fisher_p = fisher_p,
             AA_obs = AA_obs,
             AA_types = AA_types,
             GTs=GTs,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE) 
}

#prepare empty odds ratio table
odds_ratio_table <- data.frame(condition = character(), 
                               symptom = character(),
                               phenotype = character(), 
                               phenotype_tier = numeric(),
                               obs_category_w_symptom = numeric(),
                               obs_category_wo_symptom = numeric(),
                               obs_control_w_symptom = numeric(),
                               obs_control_wo_symptom = numeric(),
                               OR = numeric(), SE_OR = numeric(), 
                               UppCI = numeric(), LowCI = numeric(),
                               chi_p = numeric(), fisher_p = numeric(),
                               AA_obs = character(),
                               AA_types = character(),
                               GTs=character(),
                               stringsAsFactors = FALSE)


#create odds ratio table
for (condition in seq_along(conditions_to_test)) {
  for (icd_code in icd10_simple$ICD10code) {
    #count presence of symptom condition
    obs <- conditions_to_test[[condition]]
    #prepare which variants are the observations and their types
    var_obs <- obs[grepl(icd_code, obs$X41270),]
    
    #concatenate the vector of obs and also their consequences into two variables to print in the table
    AA_obs <- paste(var_obs$AA_change, collapse = ",")
    AA_types <- paste(var_obs$Consequence, collapse = ",")
    
    #concatenate the vector of genotypes
    GTs <- paste(var_obs$GT, collapse = ",")
    
    #calc the number of participants in each category
    freq_in_category <- nrow(var_obs)
    not_in_category <- nrow(obs) - freq_in_category
    
    control_used <- control_sets[[condition]]
    in_control <- nrow(control_used[grepl(icd_code, control_used$X41270),])
    #ntotal in set - number in damaging group - in control
    not_in_control <- nrow(control_used) - in_control
    
    
    #prepare oddsTable for each variant
    oddsTable_var <- data.frame(symptom = c(freq_in_category, in_control),
                                no_symptom = c(not_in_category, not_in_control),
                                row.names = c("group", "control"))
    
    #calculate odds ratios
    OR <- (oddsTable_var[1,1]/oddsTable_var[1,2])/(oddsTable_var[2,1]/oddsTable_var[2,2])
    SE_OR <- sqrt(1/oddsTable_var[1,1]+1/oddsTable_var[1,2]+1/oddsTable_var[2,1]+1/oddsTable_var[2,2])
    UppCI <- exp((log(OR)+1.96*SE_OR))
    LowCI <- exp((log(OR)-1.96*SE_OR))
    
    #calculate p-value with chisq and also fisher's
    #note that chi often puts error if not enough variant w symptom
    fisher_p <- fisher.test(oddsTable_var)$p.value
    chi_p <- chisq.test(oddsTable_var)$p.value
    
    new_row_to_append <- new_row(condition, icd_code)
    
    # Append the new row to the odds_ratio_table
    odds_ratio_table <- rbind(odds_ratio_table, new_row_to_append)
  }
}

#Save output
write.csv(odds_ratio_table, file = paste(date, freq, PHD1, "UKBB_all_symp_low_noelim.csv", sep = "_"), row.names = FALSE)

#eliminate rows that have no odds ratio
odds_ratio_table_no0 <- odds_ratio_table[!odds_ratio_table$OR==0,]

#####Plots#####
#by phenotype tier
if (tier == "tiered_phenotypes") {
  tier1 <- odds_ratio_table_no0[odds_ratio_table_no0$phenotype_tier == 1,]
  tier2 <- odds_ratio_table_no0[odds_ratio_table_no0$phenotype_tier == 2,]
  
  plot <- ggplot(tier1, aes(x = fct_inorder(symptom), y = OR, fill = condition)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
    labs(x = "Phenotype", y = "Odds Ratio", fill = "Score condition") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) 
  
  # save the plot to a PDF file
  pdf(paste(date, freq, PHD1, "tier1_grouped_odds_ratios.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 6, height = 5)
  print(plot)
  dev.off()
  
  plot <- ggplot(tier2, aes(x = fct_inorder(symptom), y = OR, fill = condition)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
    labs(x = "Phenotype", y = "Odds Ratio", fill = "Score condition") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  
  # save the plot to a PDF file
  pdf(paste(date, freq, "tier2_grouped_odds_ratios_noelim.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 6, height = 5)
  print(plot)
  dev.off()
  
  #Draw odds ratio as point ranges
  plot <- ggplot(tier1, aes(symptom, log(OR))) +
    geom_pointrange(
      aes(ymin = log(LowCI), ymax = log(UppCI), color = condition),
      position = position_dodge(0.9),
      size = 0.2
    )+
    scale_x_discrete(limits = unique(tier1$symptom)) +
    theme_classic() + 
    coord_flip()
  
  # save the plot to a PDF file
  pdf(paste(date, freq, PHD1, "tier1_pointrange_odds_ratios_noelim.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 12, height = 4)
  print(plot)
  dev.off()
  
  #Draw odds ratio as point ranges
  plot <- ggplot(tier2, aes(symptom, log(OR))) +
    geom_pointrange(
      aes(ymin = log(LowCI), ymax = log(UppCI), color = condition),
      position = position_dodge(0.9),
      size = 0.2
    )+
    theme_classic() + 
    scale_x_discrete(limits = unique(tier2$symptom)) +
    coord_flip()
  
  # save the plot to a PDF file
  pdf(paste(date, freq, PHD1, "tier2_pointrange_odds_ratios_noelim.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 12, height = 4)
  print(plot)
  dev.off()
  
  
} else {
  plot <- ggplot(odds_ratio_table_no0, aes(x = symptom, y = OR, fill = condition)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
    labs(x = "Phenotype", y = "Odds Ratio", fill = "Score condition") +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) 
  
  # save the plot to a PDF file
  pdf(paste(date, freq, PHD1, "grouped_odds_ratios_noelim.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 6, height = 5)
  print(plot)
  dev.off()
  
  #Draw odds ratio as point ranges
  plot <- ggplot(odds_ratio_table_no0, aes(symptom, OR)) +
    geom_pointrange(
      aes(ymin = LowCI, ymax = UppCI, color = condition),
      position = position_dodge(0.9),
      size = 0.2
    ) +
    theme_classic() + 
    coord_flip()
  
  # save the plot to a PDF file
  pdf(paste(date, freq, PHD1, "pointrange_odds_ratios_noelim.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 12, height = 4)
  print(plot)
  dev.off()
}

#What is the overlap between the test sets?
#extract low-scoring missense variants, no stops
x <- list(map = test_set$AA_change, alphaMissense = AM_test_set$AA_change, REVEL = REVEL_test_set$AA_change)
plot <- ggVennDiagram(x) + scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F4FAFE", high = "#4981BF")

# save the plot to a PDF file
pdf(paste(date, PHD1, "low_scores_venn_filtered.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 6, height = 5)
print(plot)
dev.off()

#Make same plots but log the ORs

#Make hypoparathyroidism plot
hypo_only <- odds_ratio_table_no0[odds_ratio_table_no0$symptom=="hypoparathyroidism",]

#plot with log
plot <- ggplot(hypo_only, aes(condition, log(OR))) +
  geom_pointrange(
    aes(ymin = log(LowCI), ymax = log(UppCI)),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    size = 0.2) +
  theme_classic() + 
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(from = 0, to = 14, by = 1)) +
  guides(color = FALSE)
# save the plot to a PDF file
ggsave(filename = paste(date, PHD1, "hypoparathyroidism_OR_logged.pdf", sep = "_"), plot, width = 6, height = 1.5)
print(plot)

#Make vitB12 plot
vitB12_only <- odds_ratio_table_no0[odds_ratio_table_no0$symptom=="Vitamin B12 deficiency anaemia",]

#plot with log
plot2 <- ggplot(vitB12_only, aes(condition, log(OR))) +
  geom_pointrange(
    aes(ymin = log(LowCI), ymax = log(UppCI)),
    position = position_dodge(0.9),
    size = 0.2) +
  theme_classic() + 
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks=seq(from = 0, to = 14, by = 1)) +
  guides(color = FALSE)
# save the plot to a PDF file
ggsave(filename = paste(date, PHD1, "vitB12def_OR_logged.pdf", sep = "_"), plot2, width = 6, height = 1.5)
print(plot2)
```
